    - expression: "false"
      messageExpression: "'the ConfigMap resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource. Namespace: ' + object.metadata.namespace + ', Kind: ConfigMap, Name: ' + object.metadata.name"

description: when a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget, if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance tasks and disrupt operations
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_pdb_minavailable.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy when a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget, if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance tasks and disrupt operations. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_pdb_minavailable.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.9.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: pdb-minavailable
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas || `1` }}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 0
      context:
        - name: minavailable
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | [0] | spec.minAvailable || `0`"
      validate:
        message: >-
          The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
          which is not permitted.
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
                operator: Equals
                value: "{{ minavailable }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/description: When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget, if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConditions:
    - expression: object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0
      name: has-positive-replicas
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: variables.minAvailable == 0 || variables.replicas != variables.minAvailable
      message: The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count which is not permitted.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.template.?metadata.?labels.orValue({})
      name: podLabels
    - expression: "resource.List('policy/v1', 'poddisruptionbudgets', object.metadata.namespace).items.orValue([])"
      name: pdbs
    - expression: variables.pdbs.filter(pdb, pdb.spec.?selector.?matchLabels.orValue({}).all(k, k in variables.podLabels && variables.podLabels[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]))
      name: matchingPdbs
    - expression: 'size(variables.matchingPdbs) > 0 ? int(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.?minAvailable.orValue(0)) : 0'
      name: minAvailable
    - expression: object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1)
      name: replicas

description: capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_drop_all.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_require_drop_all.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: require-drop-all
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: ALL
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.drop[].to_upper(@) || `[]` }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: 'Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back. This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the drop: ["ALL"] ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.'
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
  name: drop-all-capabilities
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(container, container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?drop.orValue([]).exists(capability, capability.upperAscii() == 'ALL'))"
      message: Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: podDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability is maintained at all times
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_pdb.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy podDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability is maintained at all times. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_require_pdb.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pdb
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.2
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment, PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      PodDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability
      is maintained at all times. This policy checks all incoming Deployments and StatefulSets
      to ensure they have a matching, preexisting PodDisruptionBudget.
      Note: This policy must be run in `enforce` mode to ensure accuracy.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: require-pdb
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
          - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
            operator: Equals
            value: CREATE
          - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
            operator: GreaterThanOrEquals
            value: 3
      context:
        - name: pdb_count
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | length(@)"
      validate:
        message: "There is no corresponding PodDisruptionBudget found for this Deployment."
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{pdb_count}}"
                operator: LessThan
                value: 1
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: 'PodDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability is maintained at all times. This policy checks all incoming Deployments and StatefulSets to ensure they have a matching, preexisting PodDisruptionBudget. Note: This policy must be run in enforce mode to ensure accuracy.'
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment, PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require PodDisruptionBudget
  name: require-pdb
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConditions:
    - expression: request.operation == "CREATE"
      name: only-on-create
    - expression: object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) >= 3
      name: replicas-gte-3
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: variables.pdb_count >= 1
      message: There is no corresponding PodDisruptionBudget found for this Deployment.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.template.?metadata.?labels.orValue({})
      name: podTemplateLabels
    - expression: resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", request.namespace).items.orValue([])
      name: allPdbs
    - expression: variables.allPdbs.filter(pdb, pdb.spec.?selector.?matchLabels.orValue({}).all(k, k in variables.podTemplateLabels && variables.podTemplateLabels[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]) && size(pdb.spec.?selector.?matchLabels.orValue({})) > 0)
      name: matchingPdbs
    - expression: size(variables.matchingPdbs)
      name: pdb_count

description: liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_probes.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_require_probes.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pod-probes
  annotations:
    pod-policies.kyverno.io/autogen-controllers: DaemonSet,Deployment,StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Pod Probes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's
      lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. For each Pod, a periodic
      `livenessProbe` is performed by the kubelet to determine if the Pod's containers
      are running or need to be restarted. A `readinessProbe` is used by Services
      and Deployments to determine if the Pod is ready to receive network traffic.
      This policy validates that all containers have one of livenessProbe, readinessProbe,
      or startupProbe defined.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-probes
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    validate:
      message: "Liveness, readiness, or startup probes are required for all containers."
      foreach:
      - list: request.object.spec.containers[]
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
            - key: livenessProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
            - key: startupProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
            - key: readinessProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. For each Pod, a periodic livenessProbe is performed by the kubelet to determine if the Pod's containers are running or need to be restarted. A readinessProbe is used by Services and Deployments to determine if the Pod is ready to receive network traffic. This policy validates that all containers have one of livenessProbe, readinessProbe, or startupProbe defined."
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Pod Probes
  name: require-pod-probes
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(container, has(container.livenessProbe) || has(container.readinessProbe) || has(container.startupProbe))
      messageExpression: "'Liveness, readiness, or startup probes are required for all containers. Missing probes in containers: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(container, !(has(container.livenessProbe) || has(container.readinessProbe) || has(container.startupProbe))).map(container, container.name).join(', ')"
  variables:

description: as application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources requested and consumed by each Pod
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_resource_limits.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy as application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources requested and consumed by each Pod. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_require_resource_limits.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-requests-limits
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Limits and Requests
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      As application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources
      requested and consumed by each Pod. It is recommended to require resource requests and
      limits per Pod, especially for memory and CPU. If a Namespace level request or limit is specified,
      defaults will automatically be applied to each Pod based on the LimitRange configuration.
      This policy validates that all containers have something specified for memory and CPU
      requests and memory limits.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-resources
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "CPU and memory resource requests and memory limits are required for containers."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(initContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"

description: a read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-ro-rootfs
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Read-Only Root Filesystem
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices, PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy;
      the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state.
      An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious binaries from writing to the
      host system. This policy validates that containers define a securityContext
      with `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-readOnlyRootFilesystem
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Root filesystem must be read-only."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - securityContext:
              readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1beta1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices, PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/description: 'A read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state. An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious binaries from writing to the host system. This policy validates that containers define a securityContext with readOnlyRootFilesystem: true.'
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Read-Only Root Filesystem
  name: require-ro-rootfs
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(container, has(container.securityContext) && has(container.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem) && container.securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem == true)
      message: Root filesystem must be read-only.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels
      of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities
      allow system-level control and should be prevented. Pod Security Policies (PSP)
      allowed a list of "good" capabilities to be added. This policy checks
      ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only
      capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: allowed-capabilities
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Any capabilities added other than NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN are disallowed.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.add[] || '' }}"
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value:
                  - NET_BIND_SERVICE
                  - CAP_CHOWN
                  - ''
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities allow system-level control and should be prevented. This policy checks ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(container, container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(capability, capability in variables.allowedCapabilities))
      message: Any capabilities added other than NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN are disallowed.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers
    - expression: "['NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'CAP_CHOWN']"
      name: allowedCapabilities

description: images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk

The policy images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be
      scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved
      registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This
      policy validates that container images only originate from the registry `eu.foo.io` or
      `bar.io`. Use of this policy requires customization to define your allowable registries.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-registries
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Unknown image registry."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
          =(initContainers):
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
          containers:
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"

description: an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: unique-ingress-host
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.      
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: check-single-host-create
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      context:
        - name: hosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[].spec.rules[].host"
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: CREATE
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ hosts }}"
    - name: check-single-host-update
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: UPDATE
      context:
        - name: allhosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[?metadata.uid!='{{ request.object.metadata.uid }}'].spec.rules[].host"
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ allhosts }}"
    - name: deny-multiple-hosts
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host | length(@)}}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 1
      validate:
        message: "An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry."
        deny: {}
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/description: An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases, these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur. This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster. It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
  name: unique-ingress-host
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConditions:
    - expression: object.spec.?rules.orValue([]).exists(r, has(r.host))
      name: has-rules-with-hosts
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - networking.k8s.io
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - ingresses
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "request.operation == 'UPDATE' || !variables.currentHosts.exists(h, h in variables.otherIngressesHosts)"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: "request.operation == 'CREATE' || !variables.currentHosts.exists(h, h in variables.otherIngressesHosts)"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: size(variables.currentHosts) <= 1
      message: An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)
      name: currentHosts
    - expression: "resource.List('networking.k8s.io/v1', 'ingresses', '').items.orValue([])"
      name: allIngresses
    - expression: variables.allIngresses.filter(ing, ing.metadata.name != object.metadata.name || ing.metadata.namespace != object.metadata.namespace).map(ing, ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)).flatten()
      name: otherIngressesHosts

description: a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source
prompt: Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source. Write the converted policy to output/nctl/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-nodeport
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the `NodePort` type.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-nodeport
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Service
    validate:
      message: "Services of type NodePort are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(type): "!NodePort"

description: mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present.
prompt: Write a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present. Write the policy to output/nctl/gen_add_default_labels.yaml.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-foo-bar-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add foo=bar Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: low
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adds the label 'foo: bar' to Pods and Services if the label is absent.
      If the label already exists, it is left unchanged.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        resources:   ["pods", "services"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: missing-foo-label
      expression: "!object.metadata.?labels[?'foo'].hasValue()"
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: {"foo": "bar"}
            }
          }

description: generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default.
prompt: Write a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default. Write the policy to output/nctl/gen_create_networkpolicy.yaml.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "By default, Kubernetes allows communications across all Pods within a cluster. The NetworkPolicy resource and a CNI plug-in that supports NetworkPolicy must be used to restrict communications. A default NetworkPolicy should be configured for each Namespace to default deny all ingress and egress traffic to the Pods in the Namespace. Application teams can then configure additional NetworkPolicy resources to allow desired traffic to application Pods from select sources. This policy will create a new NetworkPolicy resource named 'default-deny' which will deny all traffic anytime a new Namespace is created."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.15.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: NetworkPolicy
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Network Policy
  name: add-networkpolicy
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
        resources:
          - namespaces
  variables:
    - expression: object.metadata.name
      name: targetNs
    - expression: |-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("NetworkPolicy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("networking.k8s.io/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-deny",
              "namespace": string(variables.targetNs)
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "podSelector": dyn({}),
              "policyTypes": dyn(["Ingress", "Egress"])
            })
          }
        ]
      name: downstream

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT).
prompt: Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT). Write the policy to output/nctl/gen_disallow_capabilities.yaml.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: 1.30+
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/description: 'Adding capabilities beyond those listed in the Pod Security Standards baseline policy must be disallowed. The allowed capabilities are: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT. Any capability not in this list will cause the resource to be denied.'
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities Beyond Baseline
  name: disallow-capabilities-baseline
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(container, container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(capability, capability == '' || capability in variables.allowedCapabilities))"
      messageExpression: "'One or more containers in ' + object.kind + '/' + object.metadata.name + ' add capabilities beyond the allowed baseline list. Disallowed capabilities found in containers: [' + variables.violatingContainerNames + ']. Allowed capabilities are: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.'"
  variables:
    - expression: "['AUDIT_WRITE','CHOWN','DAC_OVERRIDE','FOWNER','FSETID','KILL','MKNOD','NET_BIND_SERVICE','SETFCAP','SETGID','SETPCAP','SETUID','SYS_CHROOT']"
      name: allowedCapabilities
    - expression: "object.kind == 'CronJob' ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec : (object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec : object.spec)"
      name: podSpec
    - expression: variables.podSpec.containers + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.filter(c, c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).exists(cap, !(cap in variables.allowedCapabilities))).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
      name: violatingContainerNames

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked.
prompt: Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked. Write the policy to output/nctl/gen_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace, and network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to elevate privileges. Pods should not be allowed access to host namespaces. This policy ensures fields which make use of these host namespaces are unset or set to false across Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.14.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: '!variables.podSpec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false)'
      message: Sharing the host network namespace is disallowed. The field spec.hostNetwork (for Pods), spec.template.spec.hostNetwork (for Deployments), or spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork (for CronJobs) must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: '!variables.podSpec.?hostPID.orValue(false)'
      message: Sharing the host PID namespace is disallowed. The field spec.hostPID (for Pods), spec.template.spec.hostPID (for Deployments), or spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostPID (for CronJobs) must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: '!variables.podSpec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)'
      message: Sharing the host IPC namespace is disallowed. The field spec.hostIPC (for Pods), spec.template.spec.hostIPC (for Deployments), or spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostIPC (for CronJobs) must be unset or set to false.
  variables:
    - expression: "object.kind == 'Pod'"
      name: isPod
    - expression: "object.kind == 'Deployment'"
      name: isDeployment
    - expression: "object.kind == 'CronJob'"
      name: isCronJob
    - expression: 'variables.isPod ? object.spec : (variables.isDeployment ? object.spec.template.spec : object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec)'
      name: podSpec

description: denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
prompt: Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true. Write the policy to output/nctl/gen_disallow_privileged.yaml.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and grants the container nearly all the same access as the host. This policy denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers have securityContext.privileged set to true.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(container, container.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == false)
      messageExpression: "'Privileged mode is disallowed. The following containers have securityContext.privileged set to true: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(container, container.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == true).map(container, container.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: 'has(object.spec.containers) ? object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]) : has(object.spec.template) ? object.spec.template.spec.containers + object.spec.template.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.template.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]) : object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.containers + object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])'
      name: allContainers

description: requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required.
prompt: Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required. Write the policy to output/nctl/gen_require_resource_limits.yaml.
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Requires every container in a Pod to define resources.requests.memory, resources.requests.cpu, and resources.limits.memory. CPU limits are not required. Pods missing any of these fields will be denied.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resource Requests and Memory Limits
  name: require-container-resources
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, c.?resources.?requests.?memory.hasValue())
      messageExpression: "'One or more containers are missing resources.requests.memory. Containers without it: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !c.?resources.?requests.?memory.hasValue()).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, c.?resources.?requests.?cpu.hasValue())
      messageExpression: "'One or more containers are missing resources.requests.cpu. Containers without it: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !c.?resources.?requests.?cpu.hasValue()).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, c.?resources.?limits.?memory.hasValue())
      messageExpression: "'One or more containers are missing resources.limits.memory. Containers without it: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !c.?resources.?limits.?memory.hasValue()).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

The policy labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways or triggering other functionality. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_add_default_labels.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways
      or triggering other functionality. Labels are also a very basic concept and should be
      used throughout Kubernetes. This policy performs a simple mutation which adds a label
      `foo=bar` to Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
          - Service
          - ConfigMap
          - Secret
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          labels:
            foo: bar
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways
      or triggering other functionality. Labels are also a very basic concept and should be
      used throughout Kubernetes. This policy performs a simple mutation which adds a label
      `foo=bar` to Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods", "services", "configmaps", "secrets"]
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: Object.metadata.labels{
                foo: "bar"
              }
            }
          }
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (MutatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New CEL-based API group for 1.16+ |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` | Dedicated mutating type (cluster-scoped) |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0` | CEL-based policies require ≥ 1.16 |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` | New match structure using Kubernetes-native `resourceRules` format |
| (no explicit operations) | `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]` | Must be explicit; matches original mutate rule semantics |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` | `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` | `patchStrategicMerge` is not supported in the new API; `ApplyConfiguration` with a CEL `Object{}` expression is the documented equivalent |
| `metadata: labels: foo: bar` | `Object{ metadata: Object.metadata{ labels: Object.metadata.labels{ foo: "bar" } } }` | CEL merge-patch syntax — merges the label into existing labels rather than replacing them |

input_snippet:
apiVersion : kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Resources
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.10.0-alpha.2
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.7.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class
      of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes. At a minimum,
      all Pods should specify resource requests in order to be labeled as the QoS
      class Burstable. This sample mutates any container in a Pod which doesn't
      specify memory or cpu requests to apply some sane defaults.
spec:
  background: false
  rules:
  - name: add-default-requests
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    mutate:
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]"
        patchStrategicMerge:
          spec:
            containers:
            - (name): "{{element.name}}"
              resources:
                requests:
                  +(memory): "100Mi"
                  +(cpu): "100m"
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### Conversion decisions

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (MutatingPolicy) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New policy-types API group, confirmed as `v1` from the CRD (`config/crds/policies.kyverno.io/policies.kyverno.io_mutatingpolicies.yaml` in kyverno main) |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` | `MutatingPolicy` is cluster-scoped, equivalent to the old `ClusterPolicy` |
| `kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.10.0-alpha.2` / `minversion: 1.7.0` | `kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0` / `minversion: 1.16.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/v1` is only available from 1.16+ |
| `spec.background: false` | *(omitted)* | Default for `spec.evaluation.mutateExisting.enabled` is `false`; no explicit field needed |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `resources: ["pods"]` | Standard matchConstraints form for core/v1 Pods |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions` checking `CREATE\|UPDATE` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[].operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]` | Operation filtering moves into matchConstraints; no separate matchConditions needed |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.foreach[].patchStrategicMerge` | `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` | `patchStrategicMerge` is not supported in the new API; `ApplyConfiguration` is the direct equivalent for merge-style patches |
| `foreach list: "...[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]"` | `object.spec.containers.map(...)` + `object.spec.initContainers.map(...)` + `object.spec.ephemeralContainers.map(...)` | Each container list is iterated separately using CEL `map()` |
| `+(memory): "100Mi"` / `+(cpu): "100m"` (add-if-absent) | `has(c.resources) && has(c.resources.requests) && has(c.resources.requests.memory) ? <existing> : "100Mi"` | The `+()` "add if absent" anchor is replaced with CEL `has()` guards that preserve any existing value and fall back to the default only when the field is missing |
| Optional `initContainers` / `ephemeralContainers` | `!has(object.spec.initContainers) ? [] : ...map(...)` | Guards against accessing fields that may not exist on every Pod; CEL short-circuits the `!has()` check before evaluating the map expression |
