input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ndots
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add ndots
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster
      and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases.
      This policy mutates all Pods to add the ndots option with a value of 1.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-ndots
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          dnsConfig:
            options:
              - name: ndots
                value: "1"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ndots
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add ndots
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster
      and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases.
      This policy mutates all Pods to add the ndots option with a value of 1.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            spec: Object.spec{
              dnsConfig: Object.spec.dnsConfig{
                options: [
                  Object.spec.dnsConfig.options{
                    name: "ndots",
                    value: "1"
                  }
                ]
              }
            }
          }
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (MutatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` |
| `minversion: 1.6.0` | `minversion: 1.16.0` |
| `spec.rules[]` (named rule list) | Flat `spec` — no rules array |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with explicit `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `operations: ["CREATE","UPDATE"]`, `resources: ["pods"]` |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge: {spec: {dnsConfig: {options: [...]}}}` | `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with a CEL `Object{}` expression |

**Why `ApplyConfiguration` (not `JSONPatch`):** The original `patchStrategicMerge` respects the `name` merge key on `dnsConfig.options`, so it adds/updates only the `ndots` entry without clobbering other options. `ApplyConfiguration` uses server-side apply semantics and does the same — the policy "owns" only the `ndots` list item, leaving any other options entries intact.

description: to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ns-quota
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Quota
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ResourceQuota, LimitRange
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      To better control the number of resources that can be created in a given
      Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods,
      ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended.
      This policy will generate ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources when
      a new Namespace is created.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: generate-resourcequota
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ResourceQuota
      name: default-resourcequota
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          hard:
            requests.cpu: '4'
            requests.memory: '16Gi'
            limits.cpu: '4'
            limits.memory: '16Gi'
  - name: generate-limitrange
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: LimitRange
      name: default-limitrange
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          limits:
          - default:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            defaultRequest:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 256Mi
            type: Container
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ns-quota
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Quota
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ResourceQuota, LimitRange
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      To better control the number of resources that can be created in a given
      Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods,
      ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended.
      This policy will generate ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources when
      a new Namespace is created.
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
        resources:
          - namespaces
  variables:
    - name: targetNs
      expression: object.metadata.name
    - name: downstream
      expression: |
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("ResourceQuota"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-resourcequota"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "hard": dyn({
                "requests.cpu": "4",
                "requests.memory": "16Gi",
                "limits.cpu": "4",
                "limits.memory": "16Gi"
              })
            })
          },
          {
            "kind": dyn("LimitRange"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-limitrange"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "limits": dyn([
                {
                  "default": dyn({
                    "cpu": "500m",
                    "memory": "1Gi"
                  }),
                  "defaultRequest": dyn({
                    "cpu": "200m",
                    "memory": "256Mi"
                  }),
                  "type": dyn("Container")
                }
              ])
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

---

### What changed

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (GeneratingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New dedicated policy-types API group |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: GeneratingPolicy` | Single-purpose kind for generation |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Namespace]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE]`, `resources: [namespaces]` | CEL-style admission matching replaces the old `match` block |
| `spec.rules[].generate.synchronize: true` (on each rule) | `spec.evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` (once, top-level) | Synchronization is now a policy-level setting |
| Two separate `rules[]` entries (one per resource) | One `variables.downstream` CEL list containing both resources | GeneratingPolicy collapses multiple generates into a single `generator.Apply()` call |
| `namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"` (JMESPath) | `expression: object.metadata.name` (CEL) | JMESPath templates replaced by CEL expressions |
| `generate.data` as static YAML | CEL map/list literals with `dyn()` wrappers | `dyn()` is required to produce dynamic `map[string]any` values that Kubernetes resource fields expect |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0` | GeneratingPolicy is a 1.16+ feature |

### Key structural notes

- **`dyn()`** — every value in the downstream resource definition must be wrapped in `dyn()` so CEL treats them as `dyn` (loosely typed) rather than strictly typed. Without it, CEL would reject map/list/string values assigned to `any`-typed fields.
- **`generator.Apply(namespace, resources)`** — the built-in CEL function that tells Kyverno to create the listed resources in the given namespace. The namespace string comes from `variables.targetNs` (`object.metadata.name` on the triggering Namespace).
- Both `ResourceQuota` and `LimitRange` are generated by a **single** `generate` expression, keeping parity with the original two-rule behavior.

description: the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that 
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation 
      cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods. 
spec: 
  rules: 
  - name: annotate-empty-dir
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - <(emptyDir): {}
  - name: annotate-host-path
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - hostPath:
              <(path): "*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation
      cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-emptydir-or-hostpath
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.volumes) &&
        object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.hostPath))
    - name: annotation-not-set
      expression: >-
        !has(object.metadata.annotations) ||
        !object.metadata.annotations.exists(k, k == 'cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict')
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              annotations: {
                "cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict": "true"
              }
            }
          }
raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

**Structural changes**

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (MutatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` | `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` |
| Anchor `+(annotation)` (add if absent) | `matchConditions` entry `annotation-not-set` |
| Anchor `<(emptyDir): {}` + `<(path): "*"` | `matchConditions` entry `has-emptydir-or-hostpath` |

**Two rules → one policy**

The original had two separate rules (one for `emptyDir`, one for `hostPath`) that applied the same patch. In the new model these are collapsed into a single policy: the `has-emptydir-or-hostpath` matchCondition uses CEL's `exists()` to check for either volume type in one expression, and a single `ApplyConfiguration` mutation adds the annotation.

**Anchor semantics → CEL**

- `+(annotation)` → the `annotation-not-set` matchCondition prevents the mutation from running (and overwriting) when the annotation is already present.
- `<(emptyDir): {}` / `<(path): "*"` → `object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.hostPath))` checks that at least one volume of either type exists before mutating.

description: a Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_securitycontext.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_add_securitycontext.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-securitycontext
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default securityContext
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod.
      Sometimes choosing default values for users rather than blocking is a better alternative to not impede
      such Pod definitions. This policy will mutate a Pod to set `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, and 
      `fsGroup` fields within the Pod securityContext if they are not already set.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-default-securitycontext
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          securityContext:
            +(runAsNonRoot): true
            +(runAsUser): 1000
            +(runAsGroup): 3000
            +(fsGroup): 2000
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-securitycontext
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default securityContext
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod.
      Sometimes choosing default values for users rather than blocking is a better alternative to not impede
      such Pod definitions. This policy will mutate a Pod to set `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, and
      `fsGroup` fields within the Pod securityContext if they are not already set.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
    - apiGroups: [""]
      apiVersions: ["v1"]
      operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
      resources: ["pods"]
  mutations:
  - patchType: JSONPatch
    jsonPatch:
      expression: |
        (
          !has(object.spec.securityContext) ?
          [JSONPatch{op: "add", path: "/spec/securityContext", value: {}}] :
          []
        ) +
        (
          !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot) ?
          [JSONPatch{op: "add", path: "/spec/securityContext/runAsNonRoot", value: true}] :
          []
        ) +
        (
          !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsUser) ?
          [JSONPatch{op: "add", path: "/spec/securityContext/runAsUser", value: 1000}] :
          []
        ) +
        (
          !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsGroup) ?
          [JSONPatch{op: "add", path: "/spec/securityContext/runAsGroup", value: 3000}] :
          []
        ) +
        (
          !has(object.spec.securityContext) || !has(object.spec.securityContext.fsGroup) ?
          [JSONPatch{op: "add", path: "/spec/securityContext/fsGroup", value: 2000}] :
          []
        )
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

## Conversion Summary

| Aspect | Old (`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1 MutatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion` | `kyverno.io/v1` | `policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind` | `ClusterPolicy` | `MutatingPolicy` |
| Resource matching | `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` |
| Patch format | `mutate.patchStrategicMerge` | `mutations[].patchType: JSONPatch` with CEL expression |
| "Set if absent" (`+` prefix) | Built-in patchStrategicMerge syntax | `!has()` CEL guards on each patch |
| `minversion` annotation | `1.6.0` | `1.16.0` |

## Key design decisions

**`patchStrategicMerge` → `JSONPatch`:** The `+` prefix in the old format means "only set if not already present." `patchStrategicMerge` is not supported in the new API. The idiomatic equivalent in MutatingPolicy is `JSONPatch` with CEL `!has()` guards — this is the pattern used in Kyverno's own test suite (e.g., `add-run-as-non-root` admission tests).

**Single mutation, concatenated patches:** The CEL expression returns a list of `JSONPatch` objects built by concatenating conditional sub-lists with `+`. Each sub-list is `[]` (no-op) when the field is already set, or a single-element list when the field is absent. A leading guard creates the `securityContext` object itself when it doesn't exist at all, so subsequent field-level `add` operations have a valid parent path.

description: pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have a matching taint
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_tolerations.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have a matching taint. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_add_tolerations.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-tolerations
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Tolerations
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.7.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >- 
      Pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have
      a matching taint. This policy adds the toleration `org.com/role=service:NoSchedule`
      if existing tolerations do not contain the key `org.com/role`.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: service-toleration
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "org.com/role"
        operator: AnyNotIn
        value: "{{ request.object.spec.tolerations[].key || `[]` }}"
    mutate:
      patchesJson6902: |-
        - op: add
          path: "/spec/tolerations/-"
          value:
            key: org.com/role
            operator: Equal
            value: service
            effect: NoSchedule
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-tolerations
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Tolerations
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have a matching taint.
      This policy adds the toleration `org.com/role=service:NoSchedule` if existing
      tolerations do not contain the key `org.com/role`.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  matchConditions:
    - name: skip-if-toleration-exists
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.tolerations) ||
        object.spec.tolerations == null ||
        !object.spec.tolerations.exists(t, t.key == "org.com/role")
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          (!has(object.spec.tolerations) || object.spec.tolerations == null) ?
            [
              JSONPatch{
                op: "add",
                path: "/spec/tolerations",
                value: [dyn({
                  "key": "org.com/role",
                  "operator": "Equal",
                  "value": "service",
                  "effect": "NoSchedule"
                })]
              }
            ] :
            [
              JSONPatch{
                op: "add",
                path: "/spec/tolerations/-",
                value: dyn({
                  "key": "org.com/role",
                  "operator": "Equal",
                  "value": "service",
                  "effect": "NoSchedule"
                })
              }
            ]
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### Conversion decisions

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (MutatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New CEL-native policy API group, v1 per task requirement |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` | Mutation-specific type; cluster-scoped by default |
| `kyverno-version: 1.7.1` / `minversion: 1.6.0` | both `1.16.0` | MutatingPolicy `v1` requires Kyverno 1.16+ |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[resources.kinds: Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` | Standard `matchConstraints` format with explicit `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions.any[key: "org.com/role", operator: AnyNotIn, value: tolerations[].key]` | `spec.matchConditions[skip-if-toleration-exists]` | CEL expression `!has(object.spec.tolerations) \|\| ... \|\| !object.spec.tolerations.exists(t, t.key == "org.com/role")` — policy skips entirely if the key already exists |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchesJson6902` (`op: add, path: /spec/tolerations/-`) | `mutations[].patchType: JSONPatch` with `jsonPatch.expression` | `patchesJson6902` maps directly to `JSONPatch` patch type |
| (always appended to `/-`, would fail if field absent) | Conditional branch: create array vs. append | The original would error if `spec.tolerations` didn't exist yet; the converted policy branches on `has(object.spec.tolerations)` — if missing it creates the array, if present it appends with `/-` |
| `dyn({...})` for toleration object | same | Needed because CEL `JSONPatch.value` requires an untyped (`dyn`) map for object values |

raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

---

### What changed and why

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (MutatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New CEL-native policy API |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` | Discrete mutation-only type |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` | New matching structure; explicitly declares `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, and `operations` |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` | `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` | `patchStrategicMerge` is not supported in CEL policies; `ApplyConfiguration` is the merge-style equivalent |
| `containers: - (name): "?*"` anchor pattern | `object.spec.containers.map(container, ...)` | CEL `.map()` over all containers is the idiomatic replacement for the `foreach`/anchor pattern |
| _(absent)_ | `spec.autogen.podControllers` | Auto-generates equivalent rules for Deployment, DaemonSet, StatefulSet, Job, and CronJob so the policy covers pods created by controllers, matching the intent of the original ClusterPolicy |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0` | Reflects the minimum version for the new policy type |

> **Note:** The live community policy examples in `kyverno/policies` currently use `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1alpha1`. The `v1` stable API is the target graduation. If you're running Kyverno < 1.16 stable, substitute `v1alpha1` for `v1`.

description: images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_block_stale_images.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_block_stale_images.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-stale-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Block Stale Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched.
      This policy checks the contents of every container image and inspects them for the create time.
      If it finds any image which was built more than 6 months ago this policy blocks the deployment.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit 
  rules:
    - name: block-stale-images
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited."
        foreach:
        - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
          context:
          - name: imageData
            imageRegistry:
              reference: "{{ element.image }}"
          deny:
            conditions:
              all:
                - key: "{{ time_since('', '{{ imageData.configData.created }}', '') }}"
                  operator: GreaterThan
                  value: 4380h
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-stale-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Block Stale Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched.
      This policy checks the contents of every container image and inspects them for the create time.
      If it finds any image which was built more than 6 months ago this policy blocks the deployment.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container,
          time.now() - timestamp(image.GetMetadata(container.image).config.created) <= duration("4380h")
        )
      message: "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited."
raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

---

### Conversion summary

| Field | Old (`ClusterPolicy`) | New (`ValidatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion` | `kyverno.io/v1` | `policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind` | `ClusterPolicy` | `ValidatingPolicy` |
| `kyverno.io/kyverno-version` | `1.6.0` | `1.16.0` |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion` | `1.6.0` | `1.16.0` |
| `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version` | `"1.23"` | `"1.27"` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | single field | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| Resource matching | `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, `resources` |
| Foreach iteration | `spec.rules[].validate.foreach` | CEL `all()` comprehension inline in the expression |
| Image metadata context | `imageRegistry` context block with JMESPath `imageData.configData.created` | `image.GetMetadata(container.image).config.created` via the Kyverno CEL ImageData library |
| Time comparison | `time_since('', '{{created}}', '') GreaterThan 4380h` | `time.now() - timestamp(...) <= duration("4380h")` — logic **inverted** because CEL validation expressions must return `true` to _pass_, whereas the old `deny.conditions` blocked when `true` |

description: containers which request use of an NVIDIA GPU often need to be authored to consume them via a CUDA environment variable called NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_check_nvidia_gpu.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy containers which request use of an NVIDIA GPU often need to be authored to consume them via a CUDA environment variable called NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_check_nvidia_gpu.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: check-nvidia-gpus
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check NVIDIA GPUs
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Containers which request use of an NVIDIA GPU often need to
      be authored to consume them via a CUDA environment variable called
      NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. This policy checks the containers which
      request a GPU to ensure they have been authored with this environment
      variable.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: check-nvidia-gpus
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: NotEquals
        value: DELETE
    validate:
      message: "Images which reserve NVIDIA GPUs must be built to use them."
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        context: 
        - name: imageData
          imageRegistry: 
            reference: "{{ element.image }}"
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              # If a container image calls for an NVIDIA GPU in its resources.limits, it must also
              # have been built with the CUDA environment variable `NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES`.
              - key: "NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=*?"
                operator: AnyNotIn
                value: "{{ imageData.configData.config.Env || '' }}"
              - key: "{{ element.resources.limits.\"nvidia.com/gpu\" || '' }}"
                operator: GreaterThan
                value: 0
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: check-nvidia-gpus
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check NVIDIA GPUs
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Containers which request use of an NVIDIA GPU often need to
      be authored to consume them via a CUDA environment variable called
      NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. This policy checks the containers which
      request a GPU to ensure they have been authored with this environment
      variable.
spec:
  # Audit mirrors the original validationFailureAction: Audit
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        resources:
          - pods
        # Excluding DELETE mirrors the original precondition: operation != DELETE
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
  validations:
    - message: "Images which reserve NVIDIA GPUs must be built to use them."
      # Logic: for every container, either it requests no GPU (or zero GPUs),
      # OR the image was built with the NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES env var.
      # This is the logical inverse of the original deny conditions:
      #   deny if: (nvidia.com/gpu > 0) AND (NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES NOT in image env)
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container,
          !container.?resources.?limits[?'nvidia.com/gpu'].hasValue() ||
          int(container.resources.limits['nvidia.com/gpu']) == 0 ||
          image.GetMetadata(container.image)
            .config.?Env.orValue([])
            .exists(envVar, envVar.startsWith('NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='))
        )
raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

**`/workspace/output/converted.yaml`** — key changes from the original `ClusterPolicy`:

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New CEL-based policy API |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Focused validate-only type |
| `kyverno-version: 1.6.0` | `kyverno-version: 1.16.0` | Min version for `policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Renamed field in new API |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[].resources: [pods]` | CEL policy match format |
| `preconditions: operation != DELETE` | `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` | Directly modeled in resource rule; no separate precondition needed |
| `validate.foreach` over `spec.containers` + `imageRegistry` context | `validations[].expression` using `image.GetMetadata()` CEL function | `foreach` replaced by CEL comprehensions; `imageRegistry` context replaced by the built-in `image.GetMetadata(container.image)` function |
| `configData.config.Env` | `.config.?Env.orValue([])` | Safe optional chaining in CEL |
| `deny.conditions` (JMESPath `AnyNotIn` + `GreaterThan`) | Inverted CEL `all()` expression | `deny` logic must be inverted to a passing expression; `AnyNotIn` → `!...exists()`; `GreaterThan 0` → `int(...) == 0` guard |

**CEL expression logic** (literal inverse of the original deny):
- **Original deny** fires when: `nvidia.com/gpu > 0` AND `NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES` not in image env
- **CEL validation passes** when: no GPU limit set **OR** GPU limit is 0 **OR** image env contains `NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=*`

description: a PodDisruptionBudget limits the number of Pods of a replicated application that are down simultaneously from voluntary disruptions
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_create_default_pdb.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a PodDisruptionBudget limits the number of Pods of a replicated application that are down simultaneously from voluntary disruptions. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_create_default_pdb.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: create-default-pdb
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Pod Disruption Budget
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.2
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A PodDisruptionBudget limits the number of Pods of a replicated application that
      are down simultaneously from voluntary disruptions. For example, a quorum-based
      application would like to ensure that the number of replicas running is never brought
      below the number needed for a quorum. As an application owner, you can create a PodDisruptionBudget (PDB)
      for each application. This policy will create a PDB resource whenever a new Deployment is created.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: create-default-pdb
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Deployment
    generate:
      apiVersion: policy/v1
      kind: PodDisruptionBudget
      name: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}-default-pdb"
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
      data:
        spec:
          minAvailable: 1
          selector: "{{request.object.spec.selector}}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: create-default-pdb
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Pod Disruption Budget
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A PodDisruptionBudget limits the number of Pods of a replicated application that
      are down simultaneously from voluntary disruptions. For example, a quorum-based
      application would like to ensure that the number of replicas running is never brought
      below the number needed for a quorum. As an application owner, you can create a PodDisruptionBudget (PDB)
      for each application. This policy will create a PDB resource whenever a new Deployment is created.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
  variables:
    - name: targetNs
      expression: "object.metadata.namespace"
    - name: pdbName
      expression: "object.metadata.name + '-default-pdb'"
    - name: selector
      expression: "object.spec.selector"
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, [
          {
            "kind": dyn("PodDisruptionBudget"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("policy/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": variables.pdbName
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "minAvailable": dyn(1),
              "selector": dyn(variables.selector)
            })
          }
        ])
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made:

### Conversion summary

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (GeneratingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: GeneratingPolicy` |
| `kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.2` | `kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0` |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Deployment]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with explicit `apiGroups: ["apps"]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `operations: ["CREATE"]`, `resources: ["deployments"]` |
| `{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}` | `variables.targetNs` → CEL: `object.metadata.namespace` |
| `{{request.object.metadata.name}}-default-pdb` | `variables.pdbName` → CEL: `object.metadata.name + '-default-pdb'` |
| `{{request.object.spec.selector}}` | `variables.selector` → CEL: `object.spec.selector` |
| `spec.rules[].generate.{apiVersion,kind,name,namespace,data}` | `spec.generate[].expression` using `generator.Apply()` with inline CEL object literal using `dyn()` wrappers |

**Key structural notes:**
- The flat `generate:` block with named fields is replaced by a `generate[].expression` containing a `generator.Apply(namespace, resources)` CEL call.
- All nested resource fields must be wrapped in `dyn()` to satisfy CEL's type system for dynamic map/object construction.
- `matchConstraints` replaces `match.any` and now requires explicit `apiGroups`/`apiVersions` (the old format inferred these from the kind name).

description: container daemon socket bind mounts allows access to the container engine on the node
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_disallow_cri_sock_mount.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy container daemon socket bind mounts allows access to the container engine on the node. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_disallow_cri_sock_mount.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-container-sock-mounts
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow CRI socket mounts
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Container daemon socket bind mounts allows access to the container engine on the
      node. This access can be used for privilege escalation and to manage containers
      outside of Kubernetes, and hence should not be allowed. This policy validates that
      the sockets used for CRI engines Docker, Containerd, and CRI-O are not used. In addition
      to or replacement of this policy, preventing users from mounting the parent directories
      (/var/run and /var) may be necessary to completely prevent socket bind mounts.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-docker-sock-mount
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Use of the Docker Unix socket is not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(volumes):
            - =(hostPath):
                path: "!/var/run/docker.sock"
  - name: validate-containerd-sock-mount
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Use of the Containerd Unix socket is not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(volumes):
            - =(hostPath):
                path: "!/var/run/containerd/containerd.sock"
  - name: validate-crio-sock-mount
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Use of the CRI-O Unix socket is not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(volumes):
            - =(hostPath):
                path: "!/var/run/crio/crio.sock"
  - name: validate-dockerd-sock-mount
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Use of the Docker CRI socket is not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(volumes):
            - =(hostPath):
                path: "!/var/run/cri-dockerd.sock"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-container-sock-mounts
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow CRI socket mounts
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Container daemon socket bind mounts allows access to the container engine on the
      node. This access can be used for privilege escalation and to manage containers
      outside of Kubernetes, and hence should not be allowed. This policy validates that
      the sockets used for CRI engines Docker, Containerd, and CRI-O are not used. In addition
      to or replacement of this policy, preventing users from mounting the parent directories
      (/var/run and /var) may be necessary to completely prevent socket bind mounts.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  evaluation:
    mode: Kubernetes
    background:
      enabled: true
  validations:
    - message: "Use of the Docker Unix socket is not allowed."
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.volumes) ||
        object.spec.volumes.all(vol,
          !has(vol.hostPath) ||
          vol.hostPath.path != "/var/run/docker.sock"
        )
    - message: "Use of the Containerd Unix socket is not allowed."
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.volumes) ||
        object.spec.volumes.all(vol,
          !has(vol.hostPath) ||
          vol.hostPath.path != "/var/run/containerd/containerd.sock"
        )
    - message: "Use of the CRI-O Unix socket is not allowed."
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.volumes) ||
        object.spec.volumes.all(vol,
          !has(vol.hostPath) ||
          vol.hostPath.path != "/var/run/crio/crio.sock"
        )
    - message: "Use of the Docker CRI socket is not allowed."
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.volumes) ||
        object.spec.volumes.all(vol,
          !has(vol.hostPath) ||
          vol.hostPath.path != "/var/run/cri-dockerd.sock"
        )
raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

## Conversion summary

### API / Kind
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy` |

### Enforcement mode
`validationFailureAction: Audit` → `validationActions: [Audit]`

`Audit` in the new API records violations in PolicyReports without blocking admission, matching the original behaviour. (`Deny` would be the equivalent of `Enforce`.)

### Background scanning
`background: true` → `evaluation.background.enabled: true` under the `evaluation.mode: Kubernetes` block.

### Rule consolidation
The original four rules all target the same resource (`Pod`) and perform the same type of check. Per the migration guide, these consolidate into a single `ValidatingPolicy` with four `validations` entries — one per socket path — preserving distinct failure messages.

### Pattern → CEL translation
The original pattern used Kyverno's optional-field operator (`=(field)`) and negated string match (`!/path`):

```yaml
=(volumes):
  - =(hostPath):
      path: "!/var/run/docker.sock"
```

The equivalent CEL expression guards against absent fields with `!has()` and uses `!=` for the negation:

```cel
!has(object.spec.volumes) ||
object.spec.volumes.all(vol,
  !has(vol.hostPath) ||
  vol.hostPath.path != "/var/run/docker.sock"
)
```

- `!has(object.spec.volumes)` — passes if there are no volumes (mirrors `=(volumes)` being optional)
- `.all(vol, ...)` — iterates every volume; validation passes only when the predicate holds for all
- `!has(vol.hostPath)` — passes if the volume is not a hostPath type (mirrors `=(hostPath)` being optional)
- `vol.hostPath.path != "..."` — the actual path check, inverting the original `!` prefix

### matchConstraints
`spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]`, `resources: ["pods"]`.

description: kubernetes Namespaces are an optional feature that provide a way to segment and isolate cluster resources across multiple applications and users
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_disallow_default_namespace.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy kubernetes Namespaces are an optional feature that provide a way to segment and isolate cluster resources across multiple applications and users. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_disallow_default_namespace.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-default-namespace
  annotations:
    pod-policies.kyverno.io/autogen-controllers: none
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Default Namespace
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Kubernetes Namespaces are an optional feature that provide a way to segment and
      isolate cluster resources across multiple applications and users. As a best
      practice, workloads should be isolated with Namespaces. Namespaces should be required
      and the default (empty) Namespace should not be used. This policy validates that Pods
      specify a Namespace name other than `default`. Rule auto-generation is disabled here
      due to Pod controllers need to specify the `namespace` field under the top-level `metadata`
      object and not at the Pod template level.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-namespace
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Using 'default' namespace is not allowed."
      pattern:
        metadata:
          namespace: "!default"
  - name: validate-podcontroller-namespace
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - DaemonSet
          - Deployment
          - Job
          - StatefulSet
    validate:
      message: "Using 'default' namespace is not allowed for pod controllers."
      pattern:
        metadata:
          namespace: "!default"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-default-namespace
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Default Namespace
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Kubernetes Namespaces are an optional feature that provide a way to segment and
      isolate cluster resources across multiple applications and users. As a best
      practice, workloads should be isolated with Namespaces. Namespaces should be required
      and the default (empty) Namespace should not be used. This policy validates that Pods
      and pod controllers specify a Namespace name other than `default`.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["daemonsets", "deployments", "statefulsets"]
      - apiGroups:   ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["jobs"]
  validations:
    - expression: "object.metadata.namespace != 'default'"
      message: "Using 'default' namespace is not allowed."
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

**Annotations**
- Removed `pod-policies.kyverno.io/autogen-controllers: none` (not applicable in the new API; resources are enumerated explicitly)
- Updated `policies.kyverno.io/minversion` from `1.6.0` to `1.16.0`

**Enforcement mode**
- `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]`

**Background scanning**
- `spec.background: true` → `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true`

**Rule consolidation**
- Two separate rules (`validate-namespace` for Pods, `validate-podcontroller-namespace` for pod controllers) are merged into a single policy since both enforced the identical condition. The resource scope is now expressed as three `matchConstraints.resourceRules` entries covering each API group:
  - `""` (core) / `pods`
  - `apps` / `daemonsets`, `deployments`, `statefulsets`
  - `batch` / `jobs`

**Pattern → CEL**
- `pattern: metadata.namespace: "!default"` → CEL expression `object.metadata.namespace != 'default'`

description: host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace, and network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to elevate privileges
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace, and network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to elevate privileges. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.22-1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace, and
      network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to elevate
      privileges. Pods should not be allowed access to host namespaces. This policy ensures
      fields which make use of these host namespaces are unset or set to `false`.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: host-namespaces
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      validate:
        message: >-
          Sharing the host namespaces is disallowed. The fields spec.hostNetwork,
          spec.hostIPC, and spec.hostPID must be unset or set to `false`.
        pattern:
          spec:
            =(hostPID): "false"
            =(hostIPC): "false"
            =(hostNetwork): "false"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26+"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace, and
      network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to elevate
      privileges. Pods should not be allowed access to host namespaces. This policy ensures
      fields which make use of these host namespaces are unset or set to `false`.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) == false &&
        object.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false) == false &&
        object.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) == false
      message: >-
        Sharing the host namespaces is disallowed. The fields spec.hostNetwork,
        spec.hostIPC, and spec.hostPID must be unset or set to `false`.
raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision:

---

### Conversion notes

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group for 1.16+ policy types |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Dedicated validating-only type; cluster-scoped by default |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Renamed field; `Audit` maps directly (`Enforce` would become `Deny`) |
| `spec.background: true` | *(omitted — default)* | Background scanning is enabled by default in ValidatingPolicy |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [pods]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` | ValidatingPolicy uses the Kubernetes VAP-style `matchConstraints` |
| `=(hostPID): "false"` *(optional pattern)* | `object.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) == false` | The `=()` prefix means "optional — skip if absent". In CEL, `?field.orValue(false)` returns the field value if present or `false` if absent, then checks it equals `false`. Semantics are identical. |
| `=(hostIPC): "false"` | `object.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false) == false` | Same pattern |
| `=(hostNetwork): "false"` | `object.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) == false` | Same pattern |
| `validate.message` | `validations[].message` | Moved into the per-validation entry |

The three optional-field checks are combined into a single `validations` entry with `&&` since they form one logical rule (matching the original single rule named `host-namespaces`).

description: the ':latest' tag is mutable and can lead to unexpected errors if the image changes
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_disallow_latest_tag.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy the ':latest' tag is mutable and can lead to unexpected errors if the image changes. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_disallow_latest_tag.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-latest-tag
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Latest Tag
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ':latest' tag is mutable and can lead to unexpected errors if the
      image changes. A best practice is to use an immutable tag that maps to
      a specific version of an application Pod. This policy validates that the image
      specifies a tag and that it is not called `latest`.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: require-image-tag
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "An image tag is required."
      foreach:
        - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
          pattern:
            image: "*:*"
        - list: "request.object.spec.initContainers"
          pattern:
            image: "*:*"
        - list: "request.object.spec.ephemeralContainers"
          pattern:
            image: "*:*"
  - name: validate-image-tag
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Using a mutable image tag e.g. 'latest' is not allowed."
      foreach:
        - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
          pattern:
            image: "!*:latest"
        - list: "request.object.spec.initContainers"
          pattern:
            image: "!*:latest"
        - list: "request.object.spec.ephemeralContainers"
          pattern:
            image: "!*:latest"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-latest-tag
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Latest Tag
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ':latest' tag is mutable and can lead to unexpected errors if the
      image changes. A best practice is to use an immutable tag that maps to
      a specific version of an application Pod. This policy validates that the image
      specifies a tag and that it is not called `latest`.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container, container.image.contains(':')) &&
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).all(container, container.image.contains(':')) &&
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).all(container, container.image.contains(':'))
      message: "An image tag is required."
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container, !container.image.endsWith(':latest')) &&
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).all(container, !container.image.endsWith(':latest')) &&
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).all(container, !container.image.endsWith(':latest'))
      message: "Using a mutable image tag e.g. 'latest' is not allowed."
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

### Structural changes

| Original (`ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`ValidatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with explicit `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `resources: [pods]` |
| Two `spec.rules[]` with `validate.foreach` | Two `spec.validations[]` entries with CEL expressions |

### Rule conversion (foreach + pattern → CEL comprehensions)

The original `foreach` loops over `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` using Kyverno pattern matching. These are replaced with CEL `all()` comprehensions across all three container types in a single expression:

- **`require-image-tag`** — `pattern: image: "*:*"` → `container.image.contains(':')` applied via `.all()` on each list
- **`validate-image-tag`** — `pattern: image: "!*:latest"` → `!container.image.endsWith(':latest')` applied via `.all()`

`initContainers` and `ephemeralContainers` use optional chaining (`object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])`) because those fields are optional on a Pod spec — this prevents index-out-of-bounds / field-not-found errors when they are absent.

description: privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_disallow_privileged.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_disallow_privileged.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.22-1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed. This policy
      ensures Pods do not call for privileged mode.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: privileged-containers
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      validate:
        message: >-
          Privileged mode is disallowed. The fields spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged,
          spec.initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged, and spec.ephemeralContainers[*].securityContext.privileged must be unset or set to `false`.
        pattern:
          spec:
            =(ephemeralContainers):
              - =(securityContext):
                  =(privileged): "false"
            =(initContainers):
              - =(securityContext):
                  =(privileged): "false"
            containers:
              - =(securityContext):
                  =(privileged): "false"

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed. This policy
      ensures Pods do not call for privileged mode.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          !c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false)
        ) &&
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).all(c,
          !c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false)
        ) &&
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).all(c,
          !c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false)
        )
      message: >-
        Privileged mode is disallowed. The fields spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged,
        spec.initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged, and

raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

## Conversion summary

| Original (`ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`ValidatingPolicy`) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group for CEL-based types |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Discrete single-action type |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Field renamed, now a list |
| `spec.background: true` | _(omitted)_ | Background evaluation is enabled by default in 1.16+ |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [pods]` | Explicit group/version/resource required; `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` covers admission events |
| `spec.rules[].validate.pattern` (conditional `=()` syntax) | `spec.validations[].expression` (CEL) | Pattern operators replaced with CEL optional chaining |

### CEL expression logic

The original pattern used Kyverno's `=()` conditional operator ("if field exists, it must match"). The CEL equivalent uses optional field access (`?`) and `.orValue(false)`:

```cel
# For each container type:
!c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false)
```

- If `privileged` is absent → `.orValue(false)` yields `false` → `!false` = **passes**  
- If `privileged: false` → `!false` = **passes**  
- If `privileged: true` → `!true` = **fails**

All three container arrays (`containers`, `initContainers`, `ephemeralContainers`) are checked. The optional `initContainers`/`ephemeralContainers` arrays are guarded with `.orValue([])` since they may not be present on every Pod.
