The policy resource requests often need to be tailored to the type of workload in the container/Pod. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_enforce_resources_as_ratio.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion : kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: enforce-resources-as-ratio
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Enforce Resources as Ratio
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Resource requests often need to be tailored to the type of workload in the container/Pod.
      With many different types of applications in a cluster, enforcing hard limits on requests
      or limits may not work and a ratio may be better suited instead. This policy checks every
      container in a Pod and ensures that memory limits are no more than 2.5x its requests.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: check-memory-requests-limits
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    validate:
      message: Limits may not exceed 2.5x the requests.
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              # Set resources.limits.memory equal to zero if not present and resources.requests.memory equal to 1m rather than zero
              # to avoid undefined division error. No memory request in this case is basically the same as 1m. Kubernetes API server
              # will automatically set requests=limits if only limits is defined.
            - key: "{{ divide('{{ element.resources.limits.memory || '0' }}', '{{ element.resources.requests.memory || '1m' }}') }}"
              operator: GreaterThan
              value: 2.5
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: enforce-resources-as-ratio
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Enforce Resources as Ratio
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.29"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Resource requests often need to be tailored to the type of workload in the
      container/Pod. With many different types of applications in a cluster, enforcing
      hard limits on requests or limits may not work and a ratio may be better suited
      instead. This policy checks every container in a Pod and ensures that memory
      limits are no more than 2.5x its requests.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container,
          !container.?resources.?limits.?memory.hasValue() ||
          quantity(container.resources.limits.memory).asApproximateFloat() <=
          quantity(container.resources.?requests.?memory.orValue('1m')).asApproximateFloat() * 2.5
        )
      message: "Limits may not exceed 2.5x the requests."
raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

**`/workspace/output/converted.yaml`** — key changes:

| Original (`ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`ValidatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any` + `preconditions` (CREATE/UPDATE) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` — the precondition is now expressed directly in the match constraints |
| `spec.rules[].validate.foreach` + JMESPath `divide()` | `spec.validations[].expression` with CEL `.all()` comprehension + `quantity().asApproximateFloat()` |

**CEL expression logic** mirrors the original deny condition exactly:

```cel
object.spec.containers.all(container,
  !container.?resources.?limits.?memory.hasValue() ||
  quantity(container.resources.limits.memory).asApproximateFloat() <=
  quantity(container.resources.?requests.?memory.orValue('1m')).asApproximateFloat() * 2.5
)
```

- **`!...hasValue()`** — if no memory limit is set, the container passes (same as original's `limits.memory || '0'` → 0/anything = 0 ≤ 2.5)
- **`?requests.?memory.orValue('1m')`** — safe-navigates optional fields; falls back to `'1m'` if requests.memory is absent (matching the original's `requests.memory || '1m'` guard against division by zero)
- **`asApproximateFloat() * 2.5`** — uses the Kubernetes CEL quantity library (`quantity()` is available from k8s 1.29+) to parse resource strings and do floating-point ratio comparison, avoiding the integer-truncation pitfall of `.asInteger() / .asInteger()`
- `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version` updated to `"1.29"` since that's the minimum for full quantity CEL support; `minversion` bumped to `1.16.0`
- `evaluation.background.enabled: true` added to enable background scan (the original had background checks implicit via the `BACKGROUND` precondition)

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: inject-sidecar
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Inject Sidecar Container
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment,Volume
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The sidecar pattern is very common in Kubernetes whereby other applications can
      insert components via tacit modification of a submitted resource. This is, for example,
      often how service meshes and secrets applications are able to function transparently.
      This policy injects a sidecar container, initContainer, and volume into Pods that match
      an annotation called `vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject: true`.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: inject-sidecar
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Deployment
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          template:
            metadata:
              annotations:
                (vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject): "true"
            spec:
              containers:
              - name: vault-agent
                image: vault:1.5.4
                imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
                volumeMounts:
                - mountPath: /vault/secrets
                  name: vault-secret
              initContainers:
              - name: vault-agent-init
                image: vault:1.5.4
                imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
                volumeMounts:
                - mountPath: /vault/secrets
                  name: vault-secret
              volumes:
              - name: vault-secret
                emptyDir:
                  medium: Memory
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

## Conversion decisions

### API type
`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1 MutatingPolicy`. The 1.16+ CEL-based policy types live under the `policies.kyverno.io` API group. `MutatingPolicy` is cluster-scoped like `ClusterPolicy` was.

### `spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints`
The old `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Deployment]` becomes a proper `resourceRules` entry with explicit `apiGroups: ["apps"]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `resources: ["deployments"]`, and `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]`.

### Anchor condition `(vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject): "true"` → `spec.matchConditions`
The old patchStrategicMerge "equality anchor" `(annotation): "value"` was an inline conditional that scoped the patch to pods whose **template** carried that annotation. This becomes two CEL `matchConditions`:

1. **`has-vault-inject-annotation`** — guards against missing annotations map, then checks the annotation value with the `"key" in map` idiom (safe against missing keys) and a direct equality check.
2. **`sidecar-not-already-injected`** — replaces the implicit idempotency that patchStrategicMerge gave via its `name` merge key. ApplyConfiguration is also merge-based (SSA semantics), but this guard makes the intent explicit and avoids unnecessary processing on re-admitted objects that are already injected.

### `patchStrategicMerge` → `patchType: ApplyConfiguration`
patchStrategicMerge is not supported in CEL-based policies. `ApplyConfiguration` is the direct equivalent — it uses Kubernetes Server-Side Apply merge semantics, so specifying a container/initContainer/volume by `name` merges it into the existing list rather than replacing the whole list. The CEL expression uses Kubernetes' typed `Object{}` constructors, where each field path becomes its own type namespace (e.g. `Object.spec.template.spec.containers{...}` for container entries, `Object.spec.template.spec.containers.volumeMounts{...}` for volume mounts within them).

description: generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_kasten_generate_backup.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_kasten_generate_backup.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
yaml_preview:
# This is an example rule intended to be cloned & modified to meet organizational requirements.
# The `dataprotection` label value can be changed to correspond with specific policy templates.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional
# privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required
# privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet
      with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the "kasten-data-protection-by-label"
      policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on workloads.
spec:
  evaluation:
    # Scan existing Deployments/StatefulSets at policy creation time, mirroring the
    # background-controller behaviour of the original ClusterPolicy.
    generateExisting:
      enabled: true

  # Trigger on Deployments and StatefulSets in any namespace.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ['apps']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations:  ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources:   ['deployments', 'statefulsets']

  # Only proceed when the triggering resource carries the expected label.
  # Replaces `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.selector.matchLabels` from the
  # original ClusterPolicy.
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-dataprotection-kasten-example-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        "dataprotection" in object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels["dataprotection"] == "kasten-example"

  variables:
    # Namespace of the triggering Deployment / StatefulSet (was `request.namespace`).
    - name: requestNamespace
      expression: 'object.metadata.namespace'

    # Deterministic name for the downstream Kasten Policy resource.
    - name: kastenPolicyName
      expression: 'string(variables.requestNamespace) + "-kasten-example-policy"'

    # Fetch all Kasten Policy objects from the kasten-io namespace so we can check
    # whether one protecting this namespace already exists.
    # Replaces the `apiCall` context entry + JMESPath `existingPolicy` check.
    - name: existingPolicies
      expression: 'resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io")'

    # True when a Kasten Policy with the expected name already exists.
    # Replaces `preconditions.any[0]: existingPolicy == 0`.
    - name: policyAlreadyExists
      expression: >-
        variables.existingPolicies.items.exists(p,
          p.metadata.name == variables.kastenPolicyName
        )

    # The downstream Kasten Policy resource to be created.
    # Expressed as a CEL list of resource objects for generator.Apply().
    - name: kastenPolicy
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "kind":       dyn("Policy"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name":      string(variables.kastenPolicyName),
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment":   "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "frequency": "@daily",
              "retention": dyn({
                "daily":   7,
                "weekly":  4,
                "monthly": 12,
                "yearly":  7
              }),
              "actions": dyn([
                dyn({"action": "backup"}),
                dyn({
                  "action": "export",
                  "exportParameters": dyn({
                    "frequency": "@daily",
                    "profile": dyn({
                      "name":      "test",
                      "namespace": "kasten-io"
                    }),
                    "exportData": dyn({"enabled": true})
                  })
                })
              ]),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([
                  dyn({
                    "key":      "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values":   dyn([string(variables.requestNamespace)])
                  })
                ])
              })
            })
          }
        ]

  # Generate the Kasten Policy only when one does not already exist for this namespace.
  # The ternary mirrors the original `preconditions.any[0]: existingPolicy == 0` guard:
  # if the policy is present we return `true` (no-op); otherwise we apply it.
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        !variables.policyAlreadyExists
          ? generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.kastenPolicy)
          : true
input_snippet:
# This is an example rule intended to be cloned & modified to meet organizational requirements.
# The `dataprotetion` label value can be changed to correspond with specific policy templates.
# 
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.12.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist. This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the "kasten-data-protection-by-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on workloads.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
            - Deployment
            - StatefulSet
          selector:
            matchLabels:
              dataprotection: kasten-example
    context:
    - name: dataprotectionLabelValue
      variable:
        value: "kasten-example"
    - name: kyvernoPolicyName
      variable:
        value: "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy"
    - name: existingPolicy
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies" # returns list of Kasten policies from kasten-io namespace
        jmesPath: "items[][[@.metadata.labels.\"generate.kyverno.io/policy-name\"=='{{ kyvernoPolicyName }}'] && [@.spec.selector.matchExpressions[].values[?@=='{{ request.namespace }}']]][][][][] | length(@)" # queries if a Kasten policy protecting the namespace generated by this Kyverno policy already exists 
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ existingPolicy }}"
        operator: Equals
        value: 0 # Only generate the policy if it does not already exist
    generate:
      apiVersion: config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1
      kind: Policy
      name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ dataprotectionLabelValue }}-policy"
      namespace: kasten-io
      data:   
        metadata: 
          name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ dataprotectionLabelValue }}-policy"
          namespace: kasten-io
        spec:
          comment: "Auto-generated by Kyverno"
          frequency: '@daily'
          retention:
            daily: 7
            weekly: 4
            monthly: 12
            yearly: 7
          actions:
          - action: backup
          - action: export
            exportParameters:
              frequency: '@daily'
              profile:
                name: test
                namespace: kasten-io
              exportData:
                enabled: true
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k10.kasten.io/appNamespace
                operator: In
                values:
                  - "{{ request.namespace }}"

# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional

#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet
      with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the "kasten-data-protection-by-label"
      policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on workloads.
spec:
  evaluation:
    # Scan existing Deployments/StatefulSets at policy creation time, mirroring the
    # background-controller behaviour of the original ClusterPolicy.
    generateExisting:
      enabled: true

  # Trigger on Deployments and StatefulSets in any namespace.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ['apps']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations:  ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources:   ['deployments', 'statefulsets']

  # Only proceed when the triggering resource carries the expected label.
  # Replaces `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.selector.matchLabels` from the
  # original ClusterPolicy.
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-dataprotection-kasten-example-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        "dataprotection" in object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels["dataprotection"] == "kasten-example"

  variables:
    # Namespace of the triggering Deployment / StatefulSet (was `request.namespace`).
    - name: requestNamespace
      expression: 'object.metadata.namespace'

    # Deterministic name for the downstream Kasten Policy resource.
    - name: kastenPolicyName
      expression: 'string(variables.requestNamespace) + "-kasten-example-policy"'

    # Fetch all Kasten Policy objects from the kasten-io namespace so we can check
    # whether one protecting this namespace already exists.
    # Replaces the `apiCall` context entry + JMESPath `existingPolicy` check.
    - name: existingPolicies
      expression: 'resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io")'

    # True when a Kasten Policy with the expected name already exists.
    # Replaces `preconditions.any[0]: existingPolicy == 0`.
    - name: policyAlreadyExists
      expression: >-
        variables.existingPolicies.items.exists(p,
          p.metadata.name == variables.kastenPolicyName
        )

    # The downstream Kasten Policy resource to be created.
    # Expressed as a CEL list of resource objects for generator.Apply().
    - name: kastenPolicy
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "kind":       dyn("Policy"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name":      string(variables.kastenPolicyName),
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment":   "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "frequency": "@daily",
              "retention": dyn({
                "daily":   7,
                "weekly":  4,
                "monthly": 12,
                "yearly":  7
              }),
              "actions": dyn([
                dyn({"action": "backup"}),
                dyn({
                  "action": "export",
                  "exportParameters": dyn({
                    "frequency": "@daily",
                    "profile": dyn({
                      "name":      "test",
                      "namespace": "kasten-io"
                    }),
                    "exportData": dyn({"enabled": true})
                  })
                })
              ]),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([
                  dyn({
                    "key":      "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values":   dyn([string(variables.requestNamespace)])
                  })
                ])
              })
            })
          }
        ]

  # Generate the Kasten Policy only when one does not already exist for this namespace.
  # The ternary mirrors the original `preconditions.any[0]: existingPolicy == 0` guard:
  # if the policy is present we return `true` (no-op); otherwise we apply it.
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        !variables.policyAlreadyExists
          ? generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.kastenPolicy)
          : true
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

---

### Conversion summary

| Aspect | Old (`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy`) |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion` / `kind` | `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` | `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `GeneratingPolicy` |
| Resource trigger | `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` |
| Label filter | `match.any[].resources.selector.matchLabels` | `spec.matchConditions` (CEL expression) |
| Context variables | `spec.rules[].context[].variable` | `spec.variables[].expression` (CEL strings) |
| Existence check (API call) | `spec.rules[].context[].apiCall` + JMESPath | `variables.existingPolicies` via `resource.List(...)`, filtered in CEL |
| Precondition | `preconditions.any[0]: existingPolicy == 0` | Ternary in `generate[].expression`: `!policyAlreadyExists ? generator.Apply(...) : true` |
| Resource generation | `spec.rules[].generate.data` (YAML template) | `spec.variables[].kastenPolicy` as a CEL list of objects; applied with `generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.kastenPolicy)` |
| Existing-resource scan | Handled by background controller implicitly | `spec.evaluation.generateExisting.enabled: true` |
| JMESPath template strings | `{{ request.namespace }}` etc. | CEL: `string(variables.requestNamespace)` |
| Version annotations | `1.12.x` | Updated to `1.16.0` |

### Key CEL patterns used
- **`matchConditions`** — checks `object.metadata.labels["dataprotection"] == "kasten-example"` (replaces the `matchLabels` selector).
- **`resource.List(apiVersion, resource, namespace)`** — lists all Kasten Policy objects in `kasten-io` to replicate the original `apiCall`-based existence check.
- **`dyn()`** — wraps all nested map/list values in the generated resource object, as required by CEL's strict type system for heterogeneous Kubernetes resource structures.
- **`generator.Apply(namespace, resourceList)`** — the 1.16+ CEL primitive that creates the downstream resource; returns `true` (bool) so the ternary guard compiles correctly.

yaml_preview:
# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been
# pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional
# privileges to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole is provided below.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label,
      if the policy does not already exist. Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy
      to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: false

  # Trigger on Namespace CREATE and UPDATE so label additions to existing namespaces are handled.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["namespaces"]

  # Only proceed when the namespace carries a valid dataprotection tier label.
  # Replaces the old spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.selector.matchExpressions block.
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-valid-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        'dataprotection' in object.metadata.?labels.orValue({}) &&
        object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold', 'silver', 'bronze']

  # Replaces the old spec.rules[].context block (apiCall + JMESPath).
  variables:
    - name: nsName
      expression: "object.metadata.name"
    - name: dpLabel
      expression: "object.metadata.labels['dataprotection']"
    # Fetch all Kasten policies from the kasten-io namespace.
    - name: existingPolicies
      expression: >-
        resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "Policy", "kasten-io")
    # True when a policy for this (namespace, preset) pair already exists.
    # Replaces the old preconditions check (existingPolicy == 0).
    - name: policyExists
      expression: >-
        variables.existingPolicies.exists(p,
          has(p.spec) && has(p.spec.presetRef) &&
          p.spec.presetRef.name == variables.dpLabel &&
          has(p.spec.selector) && has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(expr,
            has(expr.values) && expr.values.exists(v, v == variables.nsName)
          )
        )

  # Generate the Kasten Policy in the kasten-io namespace when no matching policy exists.
  # The ternary guards against duplicate creation (replaces preconditions: existingPolicy == 0).
  # generator.Apply(targetNamespace, resourceList) returns true on success.
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        !variables.policyExists
        ? generator.Apply("kasten-io", [
            {
              "kind": dyn("Policy"),
              "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
              "metadata": dyn({
                "name": dyn(variables.nsName + "-" + variables.dpLabel + "-backup")
              }),
              "spec": dyn({
                "comment": dyn("Auto-generated by Kyverno"),
                "paused": dyn(false),
                "actions": dyn([
                  dyn({"action": dyn("backup")})
                ]),
                "presetRef": dyn({
                  "name": dyn(variables.dpLabel),
                  "namespace": dyn("kasten-io")
                }),
                "selector": dyn({
                  "matchExpressions": dyn([
                    dyn({
                      "key": dyn("k10.kasten.io/appNamespace"),
                      "operator": dyn("In"),
                      "values": dyn([dyn(variables.nsName)])
                    })
                  ])
                })
              })
            }
          ])
        : true
input_snippet:
# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.12.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
            - Namespace
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: dataprotection 
                operator: In
                values: 
                - gold
                - silver
                - bronze
    context:
    - name: existingPolicy
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies" # returns list of Kasten policies from kasten-io namespace
        jmesPath: "items[][[@.spec.presetRef][?name=='{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}'] && [@.spec.selector.matchExpressions[].values[?@=='{{ request.namespace }}']]][][][][] | length(@)" # queries if a policy based on the dataprotection label value, covering that app namespace already exists 
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ existingPolicy }}"
        operator: Equals
        value: 0 # Only generate the policy if it does not already exist
    generate:
      apiVersion: config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1
      kind: Policy
      name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}-backup"
      namespace: kasten-io
      data:
        spec:
          comment: "Auto-generated by Kyverno"
          paused: false
          actions:
            - action: backup
          presetRef:
            name: "{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}"
            namespace: kasten-io
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k10.kasten.io/appNamespace
                operator: In
                values:
                  - "{{ request.namespace }}"

raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

---

### What changed and why

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1` GeneratingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: GeneratingPolicy` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.selector.matchExpressions` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` (resource type) + `spec.matchConditions` (label filter via CEL) |
| `spec.rules[].context[].apiCall` with JMESPath | `spec.variables` using `resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "Policy", "kasten-io")` + a CEL `exists()` expression to replicate the JMESPath query |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions` (`existingPolicy == 0`) | `variables.policyExists` bool + ternary in the `generate` expression |
| `spec.rules[].generate.data` with `{{ JMESPath }}` expressions | `generator.Apply("kasten-io", [...])` with inline CEL map; all nested values wrapped in `dyn()` as required by the CEL generator |
| `{{ request.namespace }}` | `object.metadata.name` (namespace trigger → name is the namespace) |
| `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}` | `object.metadata.labels['dataprotection']` via `variables.dpLabel` |

### Key design decisions

- **`matchConditions`** uses `'dataprotection' in object.metadata.?labels.orValue({})` for safe optional access before checking the value — avoids a nil-dereference if the label map is absent.
- **`policyExists`** uses `has()` guards at each nested level (`p.spec`, `p.spec.presetRef`, etc.) for safe traversal of arbitrary Kasten policy objects.
- **Conditional generate**: `!variables.policyExists ? generator.Apply(...) : true` — the ternary is the idiomatic way to express "only generate if absent" without preconditions; returning `true` when skipping is a no-op.
- **`synchronize: false`** — matches the original policy's default (no sync block), meaning the generated Kasten policy is not reverted if manually edited.
- The `resource.List` third argument (`"kasten-io"`) scopes the fetch to the same namespace the original `/namespaces/kasten-io/policies` API call targeted.

description: this policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy this policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.20-1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  background: false
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: limit-configmap-for-sa-developer
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        # subjects:
        # - kind: ServiceAccount
        #   name: developer
        #   namespace: kube-system
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        subjects:
        - kind: ServiceAccount
          name: another-developer
          namespace: another-namespace
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-namespace"
        - "another-namespace"
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-configmap-name-good"
        - "another-configmap-name"
    validate:
      message: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}/{{request.object.kind}}/{{request.object.metadata.name}} resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"
      deny:
        conditions:
          all:
          - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value:
            - UPDATE
            - CREATE
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.28+"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific
      ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  # validationFailureAction: Audit → validationActions: [Audit]
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  evaluation:
    # background: false → evaluation.background.enabled: false
    background:
      enabled: false

  # spec.rules[].match → matchConstraints + matchConditions
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        # The original deny conditions checked for CREATE and UPDATE;
        # moving that filter here replaces the JMESPath
        # `{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }} AnyIn [UPDATE, CREATE]` check.
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - configmaps

  # spec.rules[].preconditions (all) → matchConditions
  # All expressions must be true for the policy to apply.
  #
  # Note on subjects: the original match.any had two branches —
  #   [0] ConfigMap with no active subject filter (the 'developer' SA in kube-system
  #       was commented out), which makes this branch match ALL requesters.
  #   [1] ConfigMap restricted to SA 'another-developer' in 'another-namespace'.
  # Because branch [0] is a superset of branch [1], the effective match covers
  # ALL subjects. No subject matchCondition is therefore added.
  # To restrict to only 'another-developer', add a matchCondition:
  #   - name: target-service-account
  #     expression: >-
  #       request.userInfo.username ==
  #       'system:serviceaccount:another-namespace:another-developer'
  matchConditions:
    # Replaces: key: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}" operator: AnyIn
    - name: target-namespace
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace in ['any-namespace', 'another-namespace']
    # Replaces: key: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}" operator: AnyIn
    - name: target-configmap-name
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.name in ['any-configmap-name-good', 'another-configmap-name']

  # spec.rules[].validate.deny → validations with inverted logic.
  # The original denied when operation was CREATE or UPDATE; since matchConstraints
  # already filters for those operations, this expression unconditionally denies
  # every request that reaches this point.
  validations:
    - expression: "false"
      # Replaces: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}/{{request.object.kind}}/
      #            {{request.object.metadata.name}} resource is protected. ..."
      messageExpression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace + '/ConfigMap/' + object.metadata.name +
        ' resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource'
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### Conversion decisions

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group for CEL-based policy types |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Discrete single-action type |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Direct field rename |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` | Nested under `evaluation` in new schema |
| `spec.rules[].match` (ConfigMap, both `any` branches) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` (group `""`, `v1`, `configmaps`) | Resource kind/group/version targeting |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[1].subjects` (`another-developer`) | No matchCondition added (see comment in file) | The first `any` branch had no subject filter (it was commented out), making it a superset that matches all subjects — the second branch's subject restriction is therefore absorbed |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions[0]` (namespace AnyIn) | `matchConditions[0]`: `object.metadata.namespace in [...]` | Preconditions become CEL `matchConditions` |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions[1]` (name AnyIn) | `matchConditions[1]`: `object.metadata.name in [...]` | Same |
| `spec.rules[].validate.deny` (operation in CREATE/UPDATE) | Operations moved to `matchConstraints.operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`; validation: `expression: "false"` | Deny-rule logic inverts in CEL — since matchConstraints already gates on the operations, the expression unconditionally fails any matched request |
| `validate.message` (JMESPath template) | `validations[].messageExpression` (CEL string concat) | JMESPath `{{ }}` replaced with CEL string concatenation |

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-positive-replicas
      expression: "object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0"
  variables:
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items
    - name: podLabels
      expression: >
        object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})
    - name: matchingPdbs
      expression: >
        variables.pdbs.filter(pdb,
          pdb.spec.selector.?matchLabels.orValue({}).all(key,
            key in variables.podLabels &&
            variables.podLabels[key] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[key]
          )
        )
  validations:
    - expression: >
        variables.matchingPdbs.size() == 0 ||
        !variables.matchingPdbs.exists(pdb,
          pdb.spec.?minAvailable.orValue(0) == object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1)
        )
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
        which is not permitted.
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.9.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: pdb-minavailable
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas || `1` }}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 0
      context:
        - name: minavailable
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | [0] | spec.minAvailable || `0`"
      validate:
        message: >-
          The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
          which is not permitted.
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
                operator: Equals
                value: "{{ minavailable }}"

raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

---

## Conversion summary

### API version & kind
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

### `spec.validationFailureAction` → `spec.validationActions`
`validationFailureAction: Audit` becomes `validationActions: [Audit]`, matching the Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` field naming.

### `spec.background` → `spec.evaluation.background.enabled`
`background: false` maps to the nested `evaluation.background.enabled: false` in the new schema.

### `spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`
The old `match.any.resources.kinds: [Deployment, StatefulSet]` becomes a `resourceRules` entry with explicit `apiGroups: [apps]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, and the same `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` (operations moved out of preconditions).

### `spec.rules[].preconditions` → `spec.matchConditions`
- The `operation AnyIn [CREATE, UPDATE]` condition is now handled directly by `matchConstraints.resourceRules.operations`.
- The `replicas > 0` condition becomes a CEL `matchCondition`: `object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0`. The `?` operator with `.orValue()` safely handles the field being absent (defaulting to 1, matching Kubernetes' own default).

### `spec.rules[].context` → `spec.variables` with `resource.List()`
The JMESPath API call is replaced with three chained CEL variables:
1. **`pdbs`** — calls `resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items` to fetch all PDBs in the same namespace.
2. **`podLabels`** — extracts `object.spec.template.metadata.labels` safely (using `?` / `.orValue({})`) to avoid null-access errors in the filter.
3. **`matchingPdbs`** — filters `pdbs` to those whose `spec.selector.matchLabels` are a subset of `podLabels`, replicating the original `label_match()` JMESPath function.

### `spec.rules[].validate.deny` → `spec.validations` (logic inverted)
The old deny rule (`deny when replicas == minAvailable`) is **inverted** into a CEL validation expression (`valid when replicas != minAvailable for all matching PDBs`):

```cel
variables.matchingPdbs.size() == 0 ||
!variables.matchingPdbs.exists(pdb,
  pdb.spec.?minAvailable.orValue(0) == object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1)
)
```

This passes when there is no matching PDB, or when no matching PDB has `minAvailable` equal to the replica count. String-valued `minAvailable` (e.g. `"50%"`) naturally won't equal an integer replica count, so percentages are implicitly handled.

description: capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_drop_all.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_drop_all.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: require-drop-all
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: ALL
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.drop[].to_upper(@) || `[]` }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers +
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?drop.orValue([]).exists(capability,
            capability.upperAscii() == 'ALL'))
      message: "Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities."
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every translation decision:

---

## Conversion summary

| Original (`ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`ValidatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [pods]` |
| `preconditions: request.operation != DELETE` | Omitting `DELETE` from `operations` in `matchConstraints` (implicit — only `CREATE` and `UPDATE` are listed) |
| `foreach.list: spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]` | `spec.variables[].expression` aggregating all three lists with safe optional chaining (`?initContainers.orValue([])`) |
| `foreach.deny: ALL AnyNotIn capabilities.drop[].to_upper(@)` | `validations[].expression`: `.all(container, ...drop.orValue([]).exists(c, c.upperAscii() == 'ALL'))` — CEL comprehension replacing the deny/foreach pattern; `upperAscii()` replaces the JMESPath `to_upper` normalization |

description: define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_labels.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_labels.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment.
      A common set of labels allows tools to work collaboratively, describing objects in a common manner that
      all tools can understand. The recommended labels describe applications in a way that can be
      queried. This policy validates that the label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is specified with some value.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: check-for-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "The label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is required."
      pattern:
        metadata:

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment.
      A common set of labels allows tools to work collaboratively, describing objects in a common manner that
      all tools can understand. The recommended labels describe applications in a way that can be
      queried. This policy validates that the label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is specified with some value.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
      messageExpression: >-
        'The label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is required. Pod ' +
        object.metadata.name + ' has labels: ' +
        (has(object.metadata.labels) ? string(object.metadata.labels) : 'none')
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` (new stable CEL-based API, requires Kyverno 1.16+)
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `ValidatingPolicy` (dedicated validating type; cluster-scoped by default)
- `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` → `1.16.0`

**Spec-level fields**
| Old | New | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Renamed field on `ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` | Moved under structured `evaluation` block |

**Rule → matchConstraints**
- The `rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` block is replaced by `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`, which requires explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources`. Pods live in the core group (`""`), version `v1`, and we match `CREATE` + `UPDATE` (standard admission operations).

**Rule → validations (CEL)**
- The Kyverno pattern `app.kubernetes.io/name: "?*"` (glob meaning "at least one character") is replaced by an explicit CEL expression:
  ```cel
  has(object.metadata.labels) &&
  'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels &&
  object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
  ```
  This safely guards against missing labels map and empty string in three steps.
- The static `message` is upgraded to a `messageExpression` that includes the pod name and the actual labels found, making violations easier to diagnose.

yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pdb
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment, StatefulSet, PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      PodDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability
      is maintained at all times. This policy checks all incoming Deployments and StatefulSets
      to ensure they have a matching, preexisting PodDisruptionBudget.
      Note: This policy must be run in `enforce` mode to ensure accuracy.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    admission:
      enabled: true
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["deployments", "statefulsets"]
  # Equivalent to the original preconditions: only evaluate when replicas >= 3.
  # The CREATE operation filter is already expressed in matchConstraints above.
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-minimum-replicas
      expression: "object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) >= 3"
  variables:
    # List all PodDisruptionBudgets in the same namespace as the incoming resource.
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).?items.orValue([])
    # Count PDBs whose selector.matchLabels are a subset of the pod template labels
    # (i.e., every key-value pair in matchLabels is present in the pod template labels).
    # This replicates the JMESPath label_match() used in the original context API call.
    - name: matchingPdbCount
      expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            has(object.spec.template.metadata.labels) &&
            k in object.spec.template.metadata.labels &&
            object.spec.template.metadata.labels[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]
          )
        ).size()
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.matchingPdbCount >= 1"
      message: "There is no corresponding PodDisruptionBudget found for this Deployment or StatefulSet."
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pdb
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.2
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Deployment, PodDisruptionBudget
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      PodDisruptionBudget resources are useful to ensuring minimum availability
      is maintained at all times. This policy checks all incoming Deployments and StatefulSets
      to ensure they have a matching, preexisting PodDisruptionBudget.
      Note: This policy must be run in `enforce` mode to ensure accuracy.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: require-pdb
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
          - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
            operator: Equals
            value: CREATE
          - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
            operator: GreaterThanOrEquals
            value: 3
      context:
        - name: pdb_count
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | length(@)"
      validate:
        message: "There is no corresponding PodDisruptionBudget found for this Deployment."
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{pdb_count}}"
                operator: LessThan
                value: 1
