prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-ro-rootfs
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Read-Only Root Filesystem
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices, PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy;
      the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state.
      An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious binaries from writing to the
      host system. This policy validates that containers define a securityContext
      with `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-readOnlyRootFilesystem
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Root filesystem must be read-only."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - securityContext:
              readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-ro-rootfs
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Read-Only Root Filesystem
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices, PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy;
      the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state.
      An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious binaries from writing to the
      host system. This policy validates that containers define a securityContext
      with `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?readOnlyRootFilesystem.orValue(false) == true)
      message: "Root filesystem must be read-only."
    - expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.initContainers) ||
        object.spec.initContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?readOnlyRootFilesystem.orValue(false) == true)
      message: "Root filesystem must be read-only."
    - expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ||
        object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?readOnlyRootFilesystem.orValue(false) == true)
      message: "Root filesystem must be read-only."
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
- `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy`

**`spec.validationFailureAction: Audit`**
- Moved to `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` — the CEL-based policy uses a list here; `Audit` maps directly. (`Enforce` would map to `Deny`.)

**`spec.background: true`**
- Moved to `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true`

**`spec.rules[*].match`**
- Replaced by `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`, using explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources` fields. `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are set explicitly (the original pattern-based rule implicitly covered both).

**`spec.rules[*].validate.pattern`**
- Converted to CEL expressions in `spec.validations`. The Kyverno pattern `spec.containers[*].securityContext.readOnlyRootFilesystem: true` becomes a CEL `all()` comprehension using optional chaining (`?`) and `.orValue(false)` as a safe default — this matches the canonical community pattern for this policy.

**Container coverage expanded**
- The original pattern only iterated `containers`, but good practice (and the Kyverno CEL community policy) also checks `initContainers` and `ephemeralContainers`. Three separate `validations` entries handle each list with a guard (`!has(...)`) for lists that may be absent.

**`policies.kyverno.io/minversion`**
- Updated from `1.6.0` to `1.16.0` to reflect the minimum version that supports `policies.kyverno.io/v1`.

description: adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels
      of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities
      allow system-level control and should be prevented. Pod Security Policies (PSP)
      allowed a list of "good" capabilities to be added. This policy checks
      ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only
      capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: allowed-capabilities
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Any capabilities added other than NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN are disallowed.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.add[] || '' }}"
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value:
                  - NET_BIND_SERVICE
                  - CAP_CHOWN
                  - ''
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels
      of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities
      allow system-level control and should be prevented. Pod Security Policies (PSP)
      allowed a list of "good" capabilities to be added. This policy checks
      ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only
      capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers +
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
    - name: allowedCapabilities
      expression: "['NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'CAP_CHOWN']"
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(capability,
            capability in variables.allowedCapabilities
          )
        )
      message: >-
        Any capabilities added other than NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN are disallowed.

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**

**`spec.validationFailureAction: Audit`** → **`spec.validationActions: [Audit]`**
- The new type uses a list-based `validationActions` field.

**`spec.background: true`** — removed; background scanning is enabled by default in `ValidatingPolicy`.

**`spec.rules[].match` + `preconditions`** → **`spec.matchConstraints`**
- The `DELETE` precondition is replaced by simply only listing `CREATE` and `UPDATE` in `operations`, which is the idiomatic CEL-policy equivalent.

**`spec.rules[].validate.foreach` (JMESPath + `deny`)** → **`spec.variables` + `spec.validations` (CEL)**
- The `foreach` loop over all container lists is replaced by a CEL `variables` block that combines all three container lists into `allContainers`.
- The `deny` logic (which checked for capabilities *not* in the allowed list) is inverted into a positive CEL `all()` comprehension: every capability added must be `in` the `allowedCapabilities` list.
- Optional-chaining (`?.`) with `.orValue([])` handles containers that have no `securityContext`, `capabilities`, or `add` field, avoiding index/nil errors.

prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_image_registries.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_restrict_image_registries.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be
      scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved
      registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This
      policy validates that container images only originate from the registry `eu.foo.io` or
      `bar.io`. Use of this policy requires customization to define your allowable registries.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-registries
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Unknown image registry."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
          =(initContainers):
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
          containers:
          - image: "eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be
      scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved
      registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This
      policy validates that container images only originate from the registry `eu.foo.io` or
      `bar.io`. Use of this policy requires customization to define your allowable registries.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allowedRegistries
      expression: "['eu.foo.io/', 'bar.io/']"
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers +
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          variables.allowedRegistries.exists(registry, c.image.startsWith(registry))
        )
      message: "Unknown image registry. Images must be from eu.foo.io/ or bar.io/."
      messageExpression: >-
        "Unknown image registry. Container '" + variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          !variables.allowedRegistries.exists(registry, c.image.startsWith(registry))
        )[0].name + "' uses image '" + variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          !variables.allowedRegistries.exists(registry, c.image.startsWith(registry))
        )[0].image + "' which is not from an allowed registry (eu.foo.io/, bar.io/)."
raw_log:
The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
- `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy`

**`spec.validationFailureAction: Audit`**
- → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` (the CEL-based type uses a list; `Enforce` maps to `Deny`)

**`spec.background: true`**
- Dropped — background scanning is enabled by default in `ValidatingPolicy`. It can be explicitly controlled via `spec.evaluation.background.enabled` if needed.

**`spec.rules[].match` (Pod on CREATE/UPDATE)**
- → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [pods]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`

**`spec.rules[].validate.pattern` (container image prefix matching)**
The original pattern `eu.foo.io/* | bar.io/*` is a Kyverno wildcard; in CEL this becomes `c.image.startsWith(registry)` across all three container lists.

- The original `=(ephemeralContainers)` and `=(initContainers)` (optional fields via `=()` anchor) are handled with `object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])` and `object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])` — using CEL's optional field access (`?`) and `orValue([])` to safely handle absent fields.
- All three container lists are merged into a single `variables.allContainers` variable, then a single `all()` comprehension validates each container's image against the `allowedRegistries` list.

**`message`**
- Static fallback `message` is kept, and a dynamic `messageExpression` is added that names the first offending container and its image for clearer violation reporting.

**`policies.kyverno.io/minversion`**
- Updated from `1.6.0` to `1.16.0` to reflect the minimum version required for `ValidatingPolicy`.

description: an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: unique-ingress-host
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.      
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: check-single-host-create
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      context:
        - name: hosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[].spec.rules[].host"
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: CREATE
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ hosts }}"
    - name: check-single-host-update
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: UPDATE
      context:
        - name: allhosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[?metadata.uid!='{{ request.object.metadata.uid }}'].spec.rules[].host"
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ allhosts }}"
    - name: deny-multiple-hosts
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host | length(@)}}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 1
      validate:
        message: "An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry."
        deny: {}
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: unique-ingress-host
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - networking.k8s.io
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - ingresses
  variables:
    # All existing ingresses cluster-wide (used for both CREATE and UPDATE checks).
    # For UPDATE, we exclude the resource being updated by its UID so that its own
    # hosts do not count as conflicts with itself.
    - name: existingIngresses
      expression: >-
        resource.List("networking.k8s.io/v1", "ingresses", "").items.orValue([])
    - name: otherIngresses
      expression: >-
        variables.existingIngresses.filter(ing,
          ing.metadata.uid != object.metadata.uid
        )
    # Hosts already in use by other ingresses (excludes the resource under review).
    - name: usedHosts
      expression: >-
        variables.otherIngresses.map(ing,
          ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).map(r, r.?host.orValue(""))
        ).flatten().filter(h, h != "")
    # Hosts declared in the incoming resource.
    - name: incomingHosts
      expression: >-
        object.spec.?rules.orValue([]).map(r, r.?host.orValue("")).filter(h, h != "")
  validations:
    # Deny if more than one host is specified in the incoming Ingress.
    - expression: >-
        variables.incomingHosts.size() <= 1
      message: "An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry."
    # Deny if any incoming host is already used by another Ingress in the cluster.
    - expression: >-
        !variables.incomingHosts.exists(h, h in variables.usedHosts)
      message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

**Structural changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`
- `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]`
- `spec.background: false` → `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false`
- `spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` (with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `resources`)
- Three separate rules (CREATE rule, UPDATE rule, deny-multiple-hosts rule) → **two `validations` entries** — the new policy type handles a single validation block for all operations
- `spec.rules[].context` (JMESPath API calls) → `spec.variables` using the `resource.List()` CEL library function

**Logic changes:**
- The original had two separate rules with different API call filters (CREATE fetched all hosts; UPDATE excluded the current resource's UID via JMESPath). The CEL version uses a single `otherIngresses` variable that filters by `ing.metadata.uid != object.metadata.uid` — this naturally handles both CREATE (UID won't match anything) and UPDATE (excludes self).
- `validate.deny` with `AnyIn` operator → inverted to a CEL `!exists(h, h in ...)` expression (as documented: deny rules must have their logic inverted in CEL).
- The "deny-multiple-hosts" precondition check (`length > 1`) → a `validations` entry `incomingHosts.size() <= 1`.

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-nodeport
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the `NodePort` type.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-nodeport
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Service
    validate:
      message: "Services of type NodePort are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(type): "!NodePort"

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during migration:

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**: `kyverno.io/v1` + `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `ValidatingPolicy`.

**`spec.validationFailureAction: Audit`** → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` (list form; use `Deny` to enforce).

**`spec.background: true`** → `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true`.

description: mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_add_default_labels.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-foo-bar-label
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods", "services"]
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          !has(object.metadata.labels) ?
          [
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/metadata/labels",
              value: {"foo": "bar"}
            }
          ] :
          !has(object.metadata.labels.foo) ?
          [
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/metadata/labels/foo",
              value: "bar"
            }
          ] :
          []
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and why it's structured this way:

**Policy structure:**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` — the new Kyverno 1.16+ CEL-based policy API
- `kind: MutatingPolicy` — cluster-scoped mutating policy (supersedes `ClusterPolicy` with a `mutate` rule)
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — matches both `pods` and `services` in core API group (`""`) on `CREATE` and `UPDATE`

**Mutation logic** uses `patchType: JSONPatch` with a three-way conditional CEL expression:

1. If `metadata.labels` doesn't exist at all → add the entire `labels` map with `{"foo": "bar"}`
2. Else if `metadata.labels.foo` is absent → add just the `foo` key with value `"bar"`
3. Else (label already present with any value) → return an empty patch `[]`, leaving it unchanged

This satisfies the "add if absent, leave unchanged if already present" requirement precisely. An `ApplyConfiguration` patch would have overwritten an existing `foo` label with a different value during server-side apply reconciliation — the JSONPatch conditional approach avoids that.

description: generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_create_networkpolicy.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: generate-default-deny-networkpolicy
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE']
        resources: ['namespaces']
  variables:
    - name: nsName
      expression: 'object.metadata.name'
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("NetworkPolicy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("networking.k8s.io/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-deny",
              "namespace": string(variables.nsName)
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "podSelector": dyn({}),
              "policyTypes": dyn(["Ingress", "Egress"])
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply(variables.nsName, variables.downstream)
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and why each field is structured this way:

**Structure overview:**

- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: GeneratingPolicy` — the new CEL-based type introduced in Kyverno 1.16+/1.17 (stable), replacing `ClusterPolicy` with a `generate` rule.

- `spec.evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` — keeps the generated `NetworkPolicy` in sync with the policy; if someone manually modifies or deletes `default-deny` in a namespace, Kyverno will revert/recreate it.

- `spec.matchConstraints` — triggers on `CREATE` of any `v1/namespaces`, so every newly-created namespace fires the policy.

- `spec.variables`:
  - `nsName` — captures the new namespace's name from `object.metadata.name`
  - `downstream` — defines the full `NetworkPolicy` object as a CEL expression using `dyn()` wrappers (required for dynamic typing in CEL)

- The generated `NetworkPolicy` spec:
  - `podSelector: {}` — empty selector matches all Pods
  - `policyTypes: ["Ingress", "Egress"]` — both directions
  - No `ingress` or `egress` rules defined — results in a full default-deny for all traffic

- `spec.generate` — calls `generator.Apply(variables.nsName, variables.downstream)` to create the resource in the new namespace.

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding Linux capabilities beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline
      allow-list is disallowed. Any container, initContainer, or
      ephemeralContainer that lists a capability in
      securityContext.capabilities.add outside the approved set will be denied.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - deployments
        - cronjobs
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  variables:
    - name: allowedCapabilities
      expression: >-
        [
          'AUDIT_WRITE', 'CHOWN', 'DAC_OVERRIDE', 'FOWNER', 'FSETID',
          'KILL', 'MKNOD', 'NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'SETFCAP', 'SETGID',
          'SETPCAP', 'SETUID', 'SYS_CHROOT'
        ]

    # Resolve pod spec from Pod, Deployment (spec.template.spec),
    # or CronJob (spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec).
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod'
          ? object.spec
          : object.kind == 'Deployment'
            ? object.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec

    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
          + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
          + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])

  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(cap,
            cap == '' || cap in variables.allowedCapabilities
          )
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Container(s) in " + object.kind + "/" + object.metadata.name +
        " add capabilities beyond the PSS baseline allow-list. " +
        "Allowed: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, " +
        "MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT."
      message: >-
        Any capabilities added beyond the allowed list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
        DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
        SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT) are disallowed.
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design decisions:

**API version & kind:** `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy` as required for Kyverno 1.16+.

**Resource coverage — two approaches combined:**
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` explicitly lists Pods (`""`/v1), Deployments (`apps`/v1), and CronJobs (`batch`/v1).
- `autogen.podControllers` declares `deployments` and `cronjobs` so Kyverno can also auto-generate derived rules if preferred.

**Pod spec resolution:** A `podSpec` variable normalises the three resource shapes:
- Pod → `object.spec`
- Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec`
- CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`

**Container union:** `allContainers` merges `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` using the optional-chaining `.?field.orValue([])` CEL pattern so missing lists don't cause errors.

**Validation:** A single CEL expression walks every container and asserts every entry in `capabilities.add` is either empty or in the 13-item PSS baseline allow-list. An empty string (`''`) is included to gracefully handle blank entries.

**Action:** `Deny` (hard enforcement). Change to `Audit` if you want observation-only mode first.

**Dynamic message:** `messageExpression` provides a contextual failure message that includes the resource kind and name; `message` serves as the static fallback.

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Host namespaces (hostNetwork, hostPID, hostIPC) allow access to shared
      host-level resources and can be used to escalate privileges. Pods and
      pod-template-bearing controllers must not enable any of these fields.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups:   ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["cronjobs"]
  validations:
    # --- Pods ---
    - expression: >-
        !(object.apiVersion == "v1" && object.kind == "Pod") ||
        (
          object.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) == false &&
          object.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false)     == false &&
          object.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)      == false
        )
      message: >-
        Sharing the host namespaces is disallowed. The fields spec.hostNetwork,
        spec.hostPID, and spec.hostIPC must be unset or set to false.

    # --- Deployments ---
    - expression: >-
        !(object.apiVersion == "apps/v1" && object.kind == "Deployment") ||
        (
          object.spec.template.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) == false &&
          object.spec.template.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false)     == false &&
          object.spec.template.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)      == false
        )
      message: >-
        Sharing the host namespaces is disallowed. The fields
        spec.template.spec.hostNetwork, spec.template.spec.hostPID, and
        spec.template.spec.hostIPC must be unset or set to false.

    # --- CronJobs ---
    - expression: >-
        !(object.apiVersion == "batch/v1" && object.kind == "CronJob") ||
        (
          object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) == false &&
          object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false)     == false &&
          object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)      == false
        )
      message: >-
        Sharing the host namespaces is disallowed. The fields
        spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork,
        spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostPID, and
        spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostIPC must be unset or set to false.
raw_log:
The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every design decision made:

---

**Policy structure**

```1:69:/workspace/output/converted.yaml
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
...
```

**Key decisions based on the docs:**

| Decision | Rationale |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | The new CEL-based policy API introduced in Kyverno 1.14+ / stable in 1.17 |
| `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Cluster-scoped; denies at admission for all namespaces |
| `validationActions: [Deny]` | Hard-deny (replaces `validationFailureAction: Enforce`) |
| `matchConstraints.resourceRules` | Three separate entries — core `pods`, `apps/v1` `deployments`, `batch/v1` `cronjobs` — covering `CREATE` and `UPDATE` |
| Three `validations` entries | Each is guarded with a type-check (`!(object.kind == "X") \|\| ...`) so that when a Pod rule fires, the Deployment/CronJob expressions are trivially `true` and don't error on missing paths |
| `object.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false)` | Safe optional chaining (`?`) + `orValue` avoids an `unknown field` error when the field is absent — the exact pattern shown in the migration guide and the community CEL policy |
| Correct spec paths | Pods → `object.spec.*`; Deployments → `object.spec.template.spec.*`; CronJobs → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.*` |

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_disallow_host_path.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-path
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      HostPath volumes allow Pods to mount directories or files from the host
      node's filesystem. This can expose sensitive host data and enable privilege
      escalation. This policy denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob that
      declares a volume with the hostPath field set, regardless of the path value.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validations:
    # For Pods: check spec.volumes directly
    - expression: >-
        !(object.kind == "Pod") ||
        object.spec.?volumes.orValue([]).all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      message: >-
        HostPath volumes are forbidden. The field spec.volumes[*].hostPath must be unset.

    # For Deployments: check spec.template.spec.volumes
    - expression: >-
        !(object.kind == "Deployment") ||
        object.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([]).all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      message: >-
        HostPath volumes are forbidden. The field spec.template.spec.volumes[*].hostPath must be unset.

    # For CronJobs: check spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes
    - expression: >-
        !(object.kind == "CronJob") ||
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([]).all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      message: >-
        HostPath volumes are forbidden. The field spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes[*].hostPath must be unset.
raw_log:
The policy is written. Here's a summary of the design decisions:

**`/workspace/output/converted.yaml`**

```1:69:/workspace/output/converted.yaml
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
...
```

**Key design decisions:**

1. **`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`** — the stable GA API introduced in Kyverno 1.17 (the `v1` channel the task requires; `v1alpha1` was used in 1.14-1.15, `v1beta1` in 1.16).

2. **`validationActions: [Deny]`** — blocks the request at admission time rather than just auditing it.

3. **Three `resourceRules` entries** — explicitly matches Pods (`""`/`v1`), Deployments (`apps/v1`), and CronJobs (`batch/v1`) across CREATE and UPDATE operations.

4. **Three separate `validations`** — each uses a guard clause (`!(object.kind == "X") ||`) so only the relevant expression fires per resource type, then traverses the correct path to volumes:
   - Pods: `object.spec.?volumes`
   - Deployments: `object.spec.template.spec.?volumes`
   - CronJobs: `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?volumes`

5. **`?volumes.orValue([])`** — the optional chaining (`?`) safely handles resources with no `volumes` field, and `orValue([])` returns an empty list as the default so the `all()` comprehension trivially passes (no volumes = no hostPath volumes).

6. **`!has(volume.hostPath)`** — checks for the mere *presence* of the `hostPath` field on any volume entry, regardless of its value (even `hostPath: {path: "/"}` is caught).

description: denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_disallow_privileged.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed.
      This policy ensures that Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs do not set
      securityContext.privileged to true in any containers, initContainers, or
      ephemeralContainers.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod' ? object.spec :
        object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec :
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
          c.securityContext.privileged == false
        )
      message: >-
        Privileged mode is disallowed. containers[*].securityContext.privileged
        must be unset or false.
    - expression: >-
        !has(variables.podSpec.initContainers) ||
        variables.podSpec.initContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
          c.securityContext.privileged == false
        )
      message: >-
        Privileged mode is disallowed. initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged
        must be unset or false.
    - expression: >-
        !has(variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers) ||
        variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
          c.securityContext.privileged == false
        )
      message: >-
        Privileged mode is disallowed. ephemeralContainers[*].securityContext.privileged
        must be unset or false.
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what was produced and why each decision was made:

**Structure decisions based on the docs:**

- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new stable CEL-based type (Kyverno v1.17+), replacing the old `kyverno.io/v1` `ClusterPolicy`.
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — replaces the old `spec.validationFailureAction: Enforce`.
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — explicitly lists all three resource types (Pods in core group, Deployments in `apps/v1`, CronJobs in `batch/v1`) with `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations. No `autogen` is needed since all three are declared directly.

**CEL expression design:**

- A `variables.podSpec` variable normalises the path to the pod spec across all three resource kinds: `object.spec` for Pods, `object.spec.template.spec` for Deployments, and `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec` for CronJobs.
- Three separate `validations` entries cover `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` respectively, following the [migration guide's advice](https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/) to use CEL comprehensions (`all()`) in place of `validate.foreach`.
- Each expression uses `has()` guards before field access to avoid `unknown field` CEL errors when `securityContext` or `privileged` are absent (equivalent to the old `=(optional):` pattern syntax).
- `initContainers` and `ephemeralContainers` are guarded with a top-level `!has(...)` check since they are optional fields on the pod spec.

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-app-kubernetes-io-name-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require app.kubernetes.io/name Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices

    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a
      non-empty value. Admission is denied if the label is missing or empty.

    - Deny

          - ""
        apiVersions:

        object.metadata.?labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'].orValue('') != ''
      messageExpression: >-

The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design choices:

**Structure:**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` with `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the Kyverno 1.16+ CEL-based type
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — blocks admission rather than just auditing
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` targeting `pods` (core API group `""`) on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations

**CEL validation expression:**
```
object.metadata.?labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'].orValue('') != ''
```
- The `?labels` optional chaining safely handles Pods with no labels at all (returns absent optional instead of erroring)
- `['app.kubernetes.io/name']` uses bracket notation required for labels with dots and slashes
- `.orValue('')` converts an absent optional to an empty string
- `!= ''` catches both missing and explicitly empty values

**Messages:**
- A static `message` fallback is included alongside a dynamic `messageExpression` that includes the Pod name for clearer error output.

description: requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required.

Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-container-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resource Requests and Limits
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Requires every container in a Pod to set memory requests, CPU requests,
      and memory limits. CPU limits are not required. Admission is denied if
      any container is missing any of these three fields.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container,
          has(container.resources) &&
          has(container.resources.requests) &&
          has(container.resources.requests.memory) &&
          has(container.resources.requests.cpu) &&
          has(container.resources.limits) &&
          has(container.resources.limits.memory)
        )
      message: >-
        All containers must set resources.requests.memory,
        resources.requests.cpu, and resources.limits.memory.
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and the key design decisions:

**Structure:**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new CEL-based type (stable in Kyverno 1.17+)
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — blocks admission outright on failure (replaces `validationFailureAction: Enforce`)
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — targets Pod `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations (replaces `spec.rules[].match`)
- `validations[].expression` — a single CEL expression using the `all()` comprehension to iterate every container

**CEL expression logic:**

```cel
object.spec.containers.all(container,
  has(container.resources) &&
  has(container.resources.requests) &&
  has(container.resources.requests.memory) &&
  has(container.resources.requests.cpu) &&
  has(container.resources.limits) &&
  has(container.resources.limits.memory)
)
```

Each `has()` guard is required because CEL raises an error on missing field access — you cannot check `container.resources.requests.memory` without first confirming the parent fields exist. CPU limits are intentionally omitted per the requirements.

Before converting, look up the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples:
- Migration guide: https://kyverno.io/docs/guides/migration-to-cel/
- Policy type docs: https://kyverno.io/docs/policy-types/
- Community policy examples: https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies (look at *-vpol/, *-mpol/, *-gpol/ directories for converted examples)
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be
      scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known,
      approved registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come
      from them. This policy validates that container images only originate from the
      registry `eu.foo.io/` or `bar.io/`.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers +
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          container.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        )
      message: >-
        Images must originate from an approved registry. Only 'eu.foo.io/' and
        'bar.io/' are permitted.
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design decisions:

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`** — the stable Kyverno 1.16+ API group (not the older `kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy).

**`kind: ValidatingPolicy`** — cluster-scoped CEL-based validation, replacing the legacy `ClusterPolicy` + `validate.cel` approach.

**`validationActions: [Deny]`** — hard-blocks non-conforming Pods at admission (use `Audit` if you only want reporting).

**`matchConstraints`** — restricts evaluation to Pod `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations in the core API group.

**`variables.allContainers`** — combines `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` into a single list using `.orValue([])` for optional fields (safe when those fields are absent).

**`validations[].expression`** — uses the CEL `all()` comprehension to assert every container image starts with either `eu.foo.io/` or `bar.io/`. Any other registry causes the Pod to be denied.
